id: Policies | Bank’s Policy | Meezan Bank_chunk_2
text: and Chairman except as mentioned in above clauses. No consultancy or allied work shall be awarded to a Director or to the firm(s), institution(s) or company(ies) etc. in which he/she individually and/or in concert with other Directors of the Bank, holds substantial interest. No remuneration under this policy would paid to the Executive Directors except travel and boarding costs as per the Bank’s standard rules and regulations and relevant laws. Directors' Remuneration Policy RISK MANAGEMENT POLICY Objective of policy is to provide policy guidelines for developing a framework for the identification and management of various risks associated with operations of Meezan Bank. Scope of Risk Management: Risk management activities take place at different hierarchy levels i.e. Strategic level (Risk Appetite and Policies established by the Board of Directors), Macro level (Risk management performed by middle management) and Micro management performed by front office which ensures compliance to procedures and guidelines, etc.) Effective Risk Management Framework: It involves Policies & Procedures, Board and senior management oversight, Adequate controls & Business line accountability, MIS, risk reporting, etc. Three lines of defence is also in place to manage risks on proactive basis. Risk Appetite Statement and Risk Policies: Board has established Risk Appetite Statement encompassing various types of risks to be complied with. Further, Bank has in place number of risk policies covering various type of areas. Policies are developed keeping in view the Bank objective, best practices and prevailing laws and regulations. Management Style, Oversight and Corporate Culture: Board ensures that the Bank’s overall risk profile is maintained at prudent levels. Board Risk Management Committee reviews and recommends policies on all related risks for approval of the Board. Senior Management has constituted management committees for effective of various risks including but not limited to credit, market, liquidity, operational, compliance, Shariah and information security risks. Risk Management Group is independent from business units so as to ensure that risks are within the approved limits and are properly measured, reported and escalated. Adequate Internal Controls: Adequate system of internal controls is in place. The Bank has established clear lines of authority & responsibility for adherence to policies, procedures and limits set by the Bank. Internal Audit & Business Risk Review Department reviews the risk management activities of various areas to ensure compliance of policies and procedures. Compliance Department ensures that all directives and issued by the Bank, SBP and other agencies are complied with. Adequate MIS, Communication and Reporting: Functional Heads establish procedures, controls and systems along with effective MIS to monitor various operations for timely identification of risk exposures. Further, reports on various risks are developed and communicated to all concerned to have perspective on overall risk profile of the Bank and for necessary decision making. THE INVESTORS’ RELATIONS POLICY This policy will apply to Meezan Bank’s Board of Directors and all employees. Meezan Bank will make timely and accurate disclosures according to the criteria laid down in the relevant laws. The Bank will maintain contact with investors and analysts through roadshows, investor

metadata.source_title: Policies | Bank’s Policy | Meezan Bank

id: Policies | Bank’s Policy | Meezan Bank_chunk_3
text: necessary decision making. THE INVESTORS’ RELATIONS POLICY This policy will apply to Meezan Bank’s Board of Directors and all employees. Meezan Bank will make timely and accurate disclosures according to the criteria laid down in the relevant laws. The Bank will maintain contact with investors and analysts through roadshows, investor conferences, Analyst briefing sessions, Corporate briefing sessions, group and individual meetings, ad hoc calls, e-mail messages and other channels of communication. In compliance to the regulatory requirement of PSX, as amended from time to time, the Bank will hold Corporate Briefing Session (CBS) for its shareholders and analyst community under intimation to Pakistan Stock Exchange, at least once in a financial year. The Bank will ensure accuracy and completeness of information in financial details being shared. The Bank will hold Analyst Briefing Sessions for the Analyst community in a financial year, usually after approval of Annual accounts by the Board. No material non-public information would be shared prior to its dissemination to the Exchange and general public through PUCARS (Pakistan Unified Corporate Action Reporting System). A forward-looking comment may be given in the periodic financial reports and / or included in a subsequent press release or briefing. When a report or rumor about the Bank contains material errors, the may choose to issue an announcement to deny or clarify the report or rumor, and provide sufficient supporting information if it feels the need to do so. The designated Primary Spokespersons are: President & CEO Deputy CEO Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Meezan Bank will observe ‘Blackout Period’ prior to the announcement of its quarterly, semi-annual and full-year results. The Blackout Period would start from the end of the relevant accounting period till the announcement of financial results to the public. Bank will not comment on industry outlook, its business performance or financial results during this period. Meezan Bank shall announce a ‘Closed Period’ in accordance with the applicable laws, the announcement of interim/final results and any business decision which may materially affect the market price of the Bank’s securities. No Directors, CEO or *Executives of the Bank shall, directly or indirectly, deal in the securities the Bank in any manner during the closed period. Duration of the Closed Period would be intimated to the Directors, CEO, and other Executives and stock exchange(s). Meezan Bank recognizes the importance of Shareholders' privacy and will not disclose Shareholders' information without their consent unless required by law. Any query related to the Investor Relations Policy should be addressed to the Company Secretariat at email: investor.relations@meezanbank.com *All employees of Meezan Bank who are serving as either Group Head or holding a Key Executive position as defined in SBP Corporate Governance Regulatory Framework (CGRF) issued vide BPRD Circular No. 5 of 2021 dated November 22, 2021 (as from time to time). BANKING ON EQUALITY POLICY Meezan Bank has issued “Banking on Equality (BoE)” Policy to engender equality in banking whilst ensuring all Shariah Standards are being followed enabling the Bank to uphold its brand of being Islamic

metadata.source_title: Policies | Bank’s Policy | Meezan Bank

id: Policies | Bank’s Policy | Meezan Bank_chunk_4
text: Circular No. 5 of 2021 dated November 22, 2021 (as from time to time). BANKING ON EQUALITY POLICY Meezan Bank has issued “Banking on Equality (BoE)” Policy to engender equality in banking whilst ensuring all Shariah Standards are being followed enabling the Bank to uphold its brand of being Islamic Bank. The Policy aims to reduce the gender gap in bank staff as well as in improving the access and use of Islamic financial products & services by women. Thereby, help in reducing gender gap in financial inclusion. The Bank oversees its implementation on periodic basis, and is also self sufficient in maintaining dis-aggregated data with regard to female employees as well as female customers. Gender Pay Gap Statement PER AND IRR POLICY PROFIT EQUALIZATION RESERVE (PER) POLICY INVESTMENT RISK RESERVE (IRR) The purpose of this reserve is to maintain a certain level of Return On Investment (ROI) for Mudarabah based depositors. The parameters for Profit Equalization Reserve (PER) are as follows: PER would be maintained from the Net Income of the pool, i.e. gross income less direct expenses and losses if any. Monthly contribution into PER would not exceed 2% of Income of the pool. Accumulated balance of PER shall not exceed 30% of Bank’s equity. 50% of the balance available in PER shall be reflected as liability and 50% as reserves. The funds of PER shall only be invested in SLR eligible securities. Returns earned on these funds will also be credited into the PER account. The Profit-Sharing Ratio (PSR) for Meezan Bank as Mudarib shall not exceed 10% for managing PER. Meezan Bank may fully or partially utilize the amount of PER to improve the returns to the depositors during any period. Note : PER is a tool for streamlining profit rates and does not guarantee the capital or profit in case of loss to the based depositors. The reserve is used as a cushion against future investment losses that Mudarabah based depositors may incur due to market and credit risks of the financing and investment portfolio. The parameters for IRR are as follows: Meezan Bank may allocate an amount upto 1% of the profit available for distribution to the depositors (Rab ul Mal Share) after deduction of Mudarib share towards IRR maintained from available for distribution amongst the pool’s depositors of Mudarib share in every profit period. The IRR reflected as liability in books of bank. The funds of IRR credited into the IRR 10% for managing IRR. The investment losses, if any, incurred by the pool shall be covered from balance available in IRR. PROTECTION AGAINST HARASSMENT Meezan Bank Ltd. attracts diversified staff from various corporate, geographical, ethnic and religious backgrounds. The Bank has institutionalized mechanisms to safeguard against the risk of violation of accepted norms of conduct in the day-to-day interaction with each other. The Bank firmly believes in the fundamental importance of the promotion of trust, openness, teamwork and professionalism; aims to promote a work environment that is free from harassment and discrimination.

id: IBAN Generator | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: ion of branch and / or incorrect account number entered at your end. IBAN Validator Note: This calculator only converts the IBAN format, length and check digit integrity against the entered account number. due to incorrect IBAN input at your end. What is an IBAN number? IBAN stands for International Bank Account Number. It converts your existing account number into an internationally standardized format. It contains details about the country, bank and local account number, hence, making it globally unique and recognizable. IBAN, being an international standard for identifying bank accounts, shall facilitate the automatic processing of money transfers by improving the speed, accuracy and reliability of your payment transactions. FAQs What is the format of an IBAN? IBAN for Meezan consists of 24 characters as per the following composition: First two letters will be Country Code (e.g: PK for Pakistan) Next two digits will be check digits (validates the correctness and integrity of the bank account) Next four letter will be SWIFT Code of the Bank digits will be zeroes: 00 Next four digits will be your branch code Last 10 digits will be customer’s account number Example: Existing 10-digits MBL account number: 1234567890 & Branch code: 0101 Equivalent IBAN number: PK10MEZN0001011234567890 How can I get my IBAN? IBAN number is printed on the Bank Statements sent to customer on half yearly interval. Alternatively, you can generate your IBAN and validate any IBAN by clicking the tabs / links provided below. Does the IBAN replace existing account number? The existing account numbers will not be replaced. What are the benefits? Since it is a globally unique and standardized bank account number with embedded check digit to verify its correctness and integrity, it shall greatly enhance the speed and reliability of funds transfers. Do I need an IBAN? For what type of transactions will IBAN be used for? Currently, IBAN can be used to receive inward remittances from abroad. MeezanGPT Assalam o Alaikum! id: Titanium Cards | Debit Cards | Card Banking | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: the PIN Change option and follow the subsequent instructions. Alternatively, you may also call 21) 111-331-331 or 111-331-332 and speak to a Phone Banking Officer to have your ATM PIN changed. What happens if I forget to collect my card from the ATM? In order to provide added security, our ATMs are programmed to capture the card if the customer forgets to take it back after any transaction. Similarly, if you forget to take the cash within a pre-set time limit i.e. (20 seconds), the machine will take back the cash and your transaction will be reversed. You may also contact the branch (staff) or alternatively use ATM telephone located at the ATM vestibule to a Phone Banking Officer. What if the ATM is out of service? In the unlikely event that a particular is out of service, a message to this effect would be indicated on the screen. Various factors could cause this condition, which include telecommunication problems, hardware breakdowns, power shut downs, etc. If you encounter an out of service ATM, please proceed to the next available ATM. You may contact our 111-331-331 or 111-331-332 to locate nearest available ATMs. What should I do in case my card is lost or stolen? In case you lose your card or it gets stolen, immediately call or 111-331-332 and report the card. You are protected against fraudulent transactions made on your card from the time you notify Meezan Bank. Who should I contact if I encounter any problems abroad? MasterCard Global Customer Assistance Service helpline numbers offers instant convenience. Dial the number of the country you are travelling to for general information and reporting issues regarding your debit card. Can I use my card at ATMs besides Meezan Bank, in Pakistan and abroad? Yes, can use your card at ATMs displaying the “1 LINK/M-Net /MasterCard” logo, across Pakistan and abroad. How long can the card be used? You can make purchases and withdraw cash using the MasterCard Debit Card until the expiration date shown on the card (which is 5 years from the issuance date). If I have multiple Meezan Bank accounts, which ones will I access with my debit card? You can designate your primary account by submitting a request with 21) 111-331-331 or 111-331-332). You can access your funds available from linked account on ATMs but can only use your primary account for purchases. id: Meezan Bank Pakistan and Bank CenterCredit Kazakhstan Sign MoU to Enhance Correspondent Banking and Bilateral Trade | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: introduction: February 25th, 2026 Meezan Bank, Pakistan’s leading Islamic bank and one of the largest banks in the country and Bank CenterCredit (BCC), one of Kazakhstan’s prominent financial institutions, have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen correspondent banking relations and support the growing volume of bilateral trade between Pakistan and Kazakhstan. The signing ceremony was held on 4 February, 2026 at Serena Hotel, Islamabad, and was attended by senior leadership and key representatives from both banks. The MoU outlines a framework for collaboration in areas including trade finance, cross-border payments, and facilitation of financial services for corporate and commercial clients operating between the two markets. Through this collaboration both banks aim to improve access to banking channels, support exporters and importers, and explore new opportunities to foster economic cooperation. The agreement reflects the commitment of Meezan Bank and Bank CenterCredit to deepen regional financial integration and contribute to increased trade and investment flows Pakistan and Kazakhstan. Speaking on the occasion, Muhammad Abdullah Ahmed, Group Head, Transaction & International Banking of Meezan Bank stated: “This MoU represents an important milestone in strengthening financial ties between Kazakhstan and Pakistan. Enhanced correspondent banking cooperation will play a vital role in supporting bilateral trade and fostering closer economic relations.” Ruslan Vladimirov, President of the Bank CenterCredit remarked: “We are pleased to partner with our Pakistani counterparts to expand correspondent banking services explore new opportunities for collaboration. This partnership will help create more efficient and secure banking channels for businesses and financial institutions in both countries.” Both banks reaffirmed their commitment to operating in accordance with international regulatory, compliance, and risk management standards. About Bank CenterCredit Kazakhstan Bank CenterCredit is one of the leaders of the country’s banking system, which has been providing simple and convenient products and services in all regions of Kazakhstan since 1988.

Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharakah. Qard Basis Current Account (Pak Rupee / Foreign Currency) is based on Qard contract. The Bank can use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles. id: IIIB Certification | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login About the Course About IIIB Certificate Courses If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Why Certificate In Islamic Banking And Finance (CIBF)? We proudly present the Certificate in Islamic Banking and Finance as a unique case-study driven certificate by International Institute of Islamic Bankers, which provides you a carefully designed blend of basic principles coupled with their practical implementation. The curriculum of this course has been designed to cover all the areas of Islamic Banking and Finance. It facilitates the reader to understand the major and important concepts of Islamic Banking in an effective manner. Meezan Bank’s qualified team of professionals and Shariah scholars work hand in hand with the course developers to improve the course quality based on latest research and development. Course Contents MODULE – 01 (CONCEPT BUILDING) Islamic Banking Introduction, Background and Global Scenario The Islamic Economic System Factors of Production in Islam, Capitalism & Socialism Views of Islam on Savings & Wealth Classification of Riba Law of Contract Rules of Sale MODULE – 02 (ISLAMIC MODES OF FINANCE - l) Murabaha Documentation & Applications of Murabaha Process Flows & Calculation Introduction to Istisna Salam Import Financing Export Financing MODULE – 03 OF FINANCE - ll) Ijarah Documentation and Practical Issues Practical Application Tijarah Musharakah Diminishing Musharakah Diminishing Musharakah Documentation MODULE – 04 (DEPOSIT STRUCTURE, ISLAMIC INVESTMENTS, TAKAFUL & QUALITY ASSURANCE) Mudarabah Structure of Mudarabah Based Deposits Products Islamic Investment Funds Sukuk Project Financing Takaful Shariah Audit & Compliance in Islamic Financial Institutions Course Duration Four months Assessment Criteria IIIB accesses students through four assignments in every module and one final comprehensive online exam. These assignments which are to be submitted online comprise of short questions on conceptual understanding and practical implementation of the topics covered in the relevant module. id: Pound Savings Account | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Pound Savings Account Open a Pound Savings Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Truly Halal Savings with Competitive Returns The Riba-free Pound Savings Account is a remunerative Foreign Currency Account for Individuals. You can open and operate this account using Pounds and earn Shariah-compliant returns under the principles of Mudarabah along with other value-added benefits. Diversify and safeguard your investments against currency fluctuations. Open your Pound Savings Account with Meezan Bank today! Key Features Chequebook Issuance of chequebook in accordance with the Schedule of Charges (SOCs) Minimum Deposit Minimum investment required to open an account is £100 Routine Withdrawals Routine withdrawals up to £500 per day allowed without prior intimation Prior Notice Withdrawals of above £500 needs 1 day prior notice Profit Payment & Calculation Profit will be paid on monthly basis and calculated on daily basis Foreign Remittances Receive Foreign Remittances No Deductions No service charges if the balance maintained is low How the Pound Savings Account Works Download Fatwa On agreeing to become a Pound Saving Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharkah. Profit Sharing Ratio Bank will share 90% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor will share 10% Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 Products Tier groups Weightage Assigned Meezan Pound Savings Account Monthly 0.27 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Tenure Profit Savings Account Monthly 1.25% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates Want to Pound Savings Account Download, Print, Fill Form PDF version of our printed Account Opening Form is also available for download. You can print the form, fill your details and submit it along with the required documents at your preferred branch. Download Form Download Account Opening Forms Account Opening Form For Individual, Joint and Sole Proprietorship Accounts For Partnerships, Companies, Trust, Clubs, Societies & Associations Accounts Visit a Branch You may visit any branch of Meezan Bank and our staff will assist you in filling the Account Opening Form and opening your account. id: Islamic Agricultural Finance | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Agricultural Financing Islamic Financing for Agricultural Products If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Your Facilitation, Our Responsibility Meezan Bank is recognized as the leader and trendsetter in Islamic Banking in Pakistan by virtue of it being the first to provide a myriad of Shariah-compliant financial products for both domestic and corporate customers. Continuing this tradition, Meezan Bank has also launched Islamic Agricultural Finance to enable the farming sector to avail Shariah-compliant financing for their needs. In addition to other permissible modes of financing, the Bank offers the following specific Agricultural Financing products: Financing Schemes Meezan Tractor Assaan Meezan Bank provides a Shariah-compliant product for acquire Tractors for mechanized farming. Meezan Tractor Aasaan finance is available to: Individual Farmers (Farm & Non-Farm) Corporate Farming Tractors Requirement Financing Tenure is from 3 to 5 years. Payment frequency can be either monthly, quarterly or half yearly. Meezan Agri Assaan To provide financing opportunities on a fast track basis with maximum ease and comfort. It offers trade, participatory and rental based modes of financing. Meezan AgriAasaan is available to: Individual/Corporate Entities having Cash Collateral in shape of MBL COII’s/Certificates and Line marking be either monthly, quarterly, half yearly, or yearly. Meezan Fasal To provide financing for agricultural input requirements for farmers who may receive full payment of agri produce in advance against deferred delivery of goods on a specified future date. Meezan FaslAasaan to: Individual Farmers engaged in farm activities Corporate Farming Financing tenure is as per cropping cycle maximum up to 18 months. can be either lump sum or in tranches. Contact our Dedicated Representatives If you are require assistance, details or are interested applying for Islamic Agricultural Financing, you may contact our Agriculture Financing representatives at the following locations: Muhammad Mati Adil In Charge Agriculture Finance Agriculture Finance Unit Commercial Banking Department Meezan House, C-25, Estate Avenue, SITE, Karachi, Pakistan PABX: +92 (21) 38103500 Ext. 2403 Fax: +92 (21) 36406081 Email: mati.adil@meezanbank.com Form submitted successfully Thank you for contacting us! id: Meezan Bank Expands Correspondent Banking Network with PKR Vostro Account for Freedom Bank Kazakhstan | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: introduction: February 10th, 2026 Meezan Bank, Pakistan’s leading Islamic bank and one of the largest banks in the country has signed an agreement with Freedom Bank Kazakhstan to establish a PKR Vostro Account, marking an important milestone in strengthening correspondent banking relations between the two institutions. The signing ceremony was held on 4 February 2026 at Serena Hotel, Islamabad, and was attended by senior management and key officials from both banks. Under the agreement, Meezan Bank will provide PKR Vostro account services to Freedom Bank Kazakhstan, enabling seamless settlement of trade and commercial transactions, improved liquidity management, and enhanced operational efficiency for businesses and financial institutions operating between Pakistan and Kazakhstan. The PKR Vostro account will improve settlement efficiency and facilitate smoother cross-border banking transactions, particularly in support of trade-related payments and remittances between the two markets. Speaking at the ceremony, Muhammad Abdullah Ahmed, Group Head, Transaction & International Banking of Meezan Bank Pakistan stated: “The establishment of this PKR Vostro account highlights Meezan Bank’s continued efforts to expand its global correspondent banking footprint and strengthen cross-border payment infrastructure. This arrangement will facilitate smoother PKR settlements, enhance transaction efficiency, and enable our banking partners and business communities to benefit from secure, reliable and compliant banking channels. We believe this collaboration will play a meaningful role in supporting trade and strengthening financial connectivity between Pakistan and Kazakhstan.” Timur Agdavletov, First Deputy Chairman of the Management Board of Freedom Bank Kazakhstan added: “We are pleased to partner with Meezan Bank in establishing PKR clearing and settlement capabilities. This represents an important step in strengthening Freedom Bank’s international banking platform and enhancing our ability to support clients engaged in trade and cross-border business with Pakistan. We look forward to developing a strong correspondent relationship that delivers greater convenience, efficiency and value for businesses in both markets.” The collaboration is expected to contribute positively to bilateral trade flows and financial cooperation, while reinforcing both institutions’ commitment to international banking standards, regulatory compliance, transparency, and robust risk management practices. About Freedom Bank Kazakhstan Freedom Bank Kazakhstan JSC is a commercial bank in Kazakhstan focused on providing modern digital financial services and products to retail customers and small businesses as part of the international Freedom Holding Corp.

text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login Labbaik Travel Asaan Govt Hajj Scheme 2026 If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print GOVT HAJJ SCHEME 2026 Basic Regular Package is from Rs. 1,150,000 to Rs. 1,250,000 Regular Hajj Scheme Long package: 38 to 42 days Regular Hajj Scheme Short package: 25 to 30 days First Instalment To be submitted with the application form Rs. 500,000 Rs. 550,000 Long & Short Package (for 4 to 6 bed sharing/full room) Second To be submitted by November 03 to 15, 2025 Rs. 650,000 Optional facilities (Subject to availability - only for Makkah Mukarramah) (Below mentioned facilities will be charged on second installment) Room Type Regular PKR Double Bed (Only for families with two members) Rs. 200,000 (per person) Triple for families with three members) Rs. 67,000 (per person) Note: Qurbani is not optional and is included in the basic package according to KSA Government instruction. From August 11, 2025, both registered and unregistered applicants can apply for Hajj 2026 until the quota is filled. Biometric Verification This is to inform you that biometric verification for Hajj 2026 is mandatory and must be completed by 17th February 2026 through either of the following options: SAUDI VISA BIO APP (Mobile App) For iOS Users: Download App For Android Users (Play Store): Download App TASHEER CENTER (Physical Visit) As a last option, biometric can be completed at Tasheer Centers as per Ministry instructions. Time : Monday to Friday, 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM Pilgrims must carry : ✅ Original Passport ✅ CNIC ✅ Computerized / Photo Hajj Receipt NO EXTENSION will be granted. As per MoRA instructions, failure to complete biometric verification will result in ineligibility for Hajj 2026 . Terms and Conditions - Second Installment Collection Submit your Hajj dues to your respective bank and obtain a computerized receipt. Pilgrims who have made online payments should continue using the online portal as usual. It will not be possible to change the selected Hajj package later; therefore, choose your package carefully when submitting the second installment. When submitting the second installment, the bank may correct your data and change the package only from short to long. To change from a long to a short package, a request must be sent to the Ministry through the bank. This will only be processed subject to availability. Before the second installment, you may make any corrections to your data at your respective bank branch. For grouping issues or correction of identity card numbers, a written request must be submitted to the relevant Hajj Officer In-Charge (IT Cell) of the Ministry. If the second installment is not submitted on time, the Hajj application will be considered canceled. In case of cancellation of Hajj admission at any stage, the Ministry is authorized to make deductions in accordance with the applicable regulations. The remaining amount of canceled applications will be refunded to the applicant’s respective bank account. Hajj pilgrims are

text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Investment Banking Corporate Advisory and Structured Finance If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Innovative & Complete Financial Solutions Realizing the increasingly diverse needs of its customers for advisory and structured finance transactions, a specialized department focusing on Investment Banking services has been established at Meezan Bank that is equipped to provide comprehensive and innovative financial solutions through a diverse product offering to its clients. In a short span of time, the Bank has established itself as a market leader in the Investment Banking market of Pakistan through providing Shariah-compliant Investment Banking services. The Bank has developed core expertise in arranging syndicated, structured and Project finance and providing advice on mergers and acquisitions, buy-side / sell-side advisory, financial restructuring, structured equity and venture capital investments. Meezan Bank’s Investment Banking team, with highly qualified and experienced professionals including ACAs, CFA charter-holders, ACCAs and MBAs, offers its specialized services across the spectrum of Investment Banking activities, which includes and Project finance transactions and providing financial advisory services for the following: Syndicate Finance Long and Short Term Finance We facilitate corporations to raise large ticket financing requirements by forming a consortium of financial institutions. We offer numerous financing solutions for our clients to fulfill their long-term as well as short-term financing requirements. Project Finance Solutions for Green Field/Brown Field Projects We provide complete financing solutions to green field / brown field projects, with limited or no recourse basis. Bank’s Investment Banking department has a proven track record of successfully executing numerous Project Finance transactions and to date has arranged various project finance deals of over Rs. 100 billion. Meezan Bank, also has the credit of structuring and arranging the first ever syndicated Islamic Project Finance Facility and first ever private to private power project in Pakistan. Sukuks Finance for Private & Listed Sukuks Depending upon customers’ requirements, we provide arrangement, shariah structuring / advisory, underwriting and placement services for privately placed and listed sukuks. Meezan Bank the credit of advising the first ever series of short-term Sukuk and arranging for first ever Sukuk backed Project financing in Pakistan. We are also acting as Joint Financial Advisor to GoP for the Sovereign Sukuk program. Structured Finance Creative Finance Solutions Structured finance involves highly complex financial transactions. It is useful in cases where standard financing products do not meet a company’s unique requirements. Investment Banking department offers creatively structured transactions including off balance sheet working capital and capital expenditure financing to fulfill our customers’ business needs. Financial Advisory Services Shariah-compliant Advisory Products Our specialist team offers a wide range of equity and advisory products including innovative shariah compliant solutions for : Mergers Acquisitions Buy-side / sell-side advisory Financial Restructuring Structured Equity Initial Public Offerings Private Placement of Equity Venture Capital Investments Underwriting Secure Issue of Securities Underwriting involves the Bank absorbing the risk of under-subscription in relation to new and secondary issues. id: Express Savings Account | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Express Savings Account Open an Express Savings Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Designed to facilitate the beneficiaries of home remittance Meezan Express Savings Account has been designed of home remittance and encourage them to use formal banking channels. The biggest advantage of Meezan Express Savings Accounts is it can be opened on the basis of a CNIC only* & there is NO WITHHOLDING TAX on cash withdrawals through cheque or ATM. *Terms and Conditions apply Meezan Express Savings account can be opened in PKR only Outward Foreign remittances are not allowed from Express Savings account Savings account can only be fed through home remittances, no other credits are allowed Debit cards issued on Meezan Express Savings accounts can only be used locally (Pay Pak Debit Cards) Key Features Chequebook Issuance of chequebook in accordance with the Schedule of Charges (SOCs) Free Pay Orders Pay Orders Free Internet Banking Free Internet Banking facility Profit Payment & Calculation Profit will be paid on monthly basis and calculated on daily basis Online Branch Banking Free Online banking service at all Meezan Bank branches Withdrawals No Restriction on withdrawals amount How the Express Savings Account Works Download Fatwa On agreeing to become a Rupee Meezan Express Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharkah. Profit Sharing Ratio Bank will share 50% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 September Tier Groups Weightage Assigned Express Savings Account Monthly 0.45 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Tenure Profit Assigned Express Saving Account Monthly 6.34% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates Want to an Express Savings Account? text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Meezan Kids Club Account Open a Kids Club Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print It’s Never too Early to Save! Are you still not 12 years old? Then Meezan Bank has something very special for you; a chance of having your own Mudarabah-based Meezan Kids Club Account. You can now put all your pocket money, Eidi and all your other cash in this account, where it will be safe and invested in line with Islamic principles.When you get this account opened, you will get to learn a lot more about managing your savings. We will also make sure you have fun while learning to save the right way. Key Features Personalized Chequebook Issuance of chequebook in accordance with the Schedule of Charges (SOCs) Free Pay Orders Free Pay Orders Minimium Investment Open your account with Rs 500/- only Profit Higher Expected Returns Age Group Specifically for kids under the age of 12 years Great Gifts Exciting gifts*** on account opening Halal Profits Halal profit every month Personalized Debit Card Free* Visa** Debit Card in different colors for boys and girls Internet and Online Banking Free Online Banking service and Internet Banking facility SMS Alerts SMS alerts on your transactions Upgrade Account Upgrade to a Meezan Teens Club Account after your 12th birthday * FREE for first issuance only Annual fee for subsequent years will be applicable as per SOC ** Paypak Card also available on this account, however charges as per SOC *** Gifts on account opening are subject to availability. Meezan Bank doesn't have any binding obligation to provide the gifts in case of unavailability. How the Kids Club Account Works Download Fatwa On agreeing to become a Kids Club Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharkah. id: Asaan Account | Remittance Account | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Meezan Asaan Remittance Account Asaan Remittance Account is a specialized banking solution designed to provide remittance beneficiaries with a secure and convenient way to receive funds. It is available in both Current and Savings accounts, allowing beneficiaries to deposit their remittances directly into their bank accounts instead of collecting cash over the counter. With higher transaction limits and seamless accessibility, it promotes financial inclusion while ensuring safety and ease of use. NOTE: Resident Pakistanis having valid Identity Document i.e., CNIC, are eligible for only one Meezan Asaan Remittance Account. This account is exclusively available to new customers. Key Features & Benefits Remittance Account is available in PKR currency only and can be opened as either a Current or Savings account No initial deposit requirement Maximum credit balance limit: PKR 3,000,000* Daily cash withdrawal limit: PKR 500,000 Daily fund transfer limit: PKR 500,000 Monthly local credit limit: PKR 1,000,000 Profit is calculated daily and paid monthly (applicable to Savings Account only) Free online banking, free Pay Orders, Free Internet and Mobile Banking Free cheque book (for Current Account only) * Foreign remittances of a personal nature allowed up to PKR 3,000,000 per month (commercial remittances not allowed) How does Asaan Remittance Account work? Remittance Account is offered in both current & savings accounts under the Islamic concept of Qard & Mudarabah. Mudarabah Basis On agreeing to become a Pak Rupee Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharakah. Qard Basis Current Account (Pak Rupee) is based on Qard contract. The Bank can use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) Want to Open a Asaan Remittance Account? Download, Print, Fill Form PDF version of our printed Account Opening Form is also available for download. You can print the form, fill your details and submit it along with the required documents at your preferred branch. Download Form Visit a Branch You may visit any branch of Meezan Bank and our staff will assist you in filling the Account Opening Form and opening your account. id: Meezan Roshan Pension Plan | RDA | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print PENSION PLAN FOR ROSHAN DIGITAL ACCOUNT CUSTOMERS* Retirement planning is one of the most important life-stage goals! With Roshan Pension Plan (RPP), Overseas Pakistanis can start preparing for their retirement today through their Roshan Digital Account (RDA). Being offered for the first time, the Roshan Pension Plan (RPP) gives Overseas Pakistanis the benefit to plan their retirement at their convenience from anywhere around the globe and enjoy exclusive plans with competitive returns. The Roshan Pension Plan is a defined self-contributory pension scheme open to all Overseas Pakistanis. Under Pension Plan (RPP), all employed and self-employed individuals can voluntarily contribute to a pension fund starting with as low as PKR 10,000/- during their working life to receive regular income after retirement. *All investments in Roshan Pension Plan are managed by Al-Meezan Investment Management Limited (AMIM) under their Meezan Tahafuz Pension Fund (MTPF) while Meezan Bank is acting as a distributor to the same. Eligibility Criteria Pension Plan (RPP), under Voluntary Pension Scheme (VPS), is a personalized and self-contributory savings plus investment to all Overseas Pakistanis that offers higher expected long-term returns according to the risk-taking ability of the investors. The following requirements must be met to contribute to the pension program: Roshan Digital Accountholders Employed or self-employed individuals, above 18 years of age. Individuals with Computerized National Identity Card/ National Identity Card for Overseas Pakistanis (CNIC/NICOP). Key Features & Benefits A convenient investment mechanism that offers long-term growth and capital appreciation solution for a comfortable post retirement life. An individualized pension account in customer’s name. Opportunity to choose asset allocation as per one’s risk appetite (Three available allocation schemes based on three Sub-Funds). Option to invest as per convenience without any risk of penalties on missing payments. Exclusive plans with zero front-end load. Only up-to 1.5% Management Fee, to be charged by AMIM. Invest in Pension Plan Terms & Conditions 18 years of age having a Roshan Digital PKR account. Minimum investment balance of PKR 10,000/-* OR Pension fund starting from PKR 10,000/- during the applicant’s working life. 25 years maximum period of investment, locked from the date of initial investment or between the age of 60 to 70 years of the applicant. A participant at any time before retirement shall be entitled to redeem the total or part of his/her accumulation subject to payment of tax @ of his/her average tax rate of the preceding three years. Pension Schemes Customers May Choose or Switch Among the Following Three Allocation Schemes to Suit Their Needs EQUITY SUB FUND INCOME SUB FUND MONEY MARKET SUB FUND Medium Volatility 50% 40% 10% Low Volatility 25% 60% 15% Lower Volatility NIL 50% 50% Risk Disclaimer : All investments in Mutual Funds & Pension Funds are subject to market risks. id: Deposit Protection Mechanism | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Deposit Protection Mechanism The Deposit Protection Corporation (DPC) has been established under the Deposit Protection Corporation Act, 2016 as a subsidiary of the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP). The primary objective of DPC is to protect small depositors for the losses incurred by them to the extent of protected amount, in the event of failure of a bank through establishment of Deposit Protection (DP) Waqf Fund. The current limit of protected amount for all eligible depositors is up to PKR 1,000,000/- (Rupees one million) per depositor per bank. All scheduled banks operating in Pakistan are mandatory member institutions of deposit protection scheme. For more details, please visit www.dpc.org.pk Shariah-compliant Deposit Protection Mechanism for Islamic Banking Institutions Under Sections 7 (3) of Corporation Act, 2016 In terms of Section 7 (3) of the Act, a Shariah-compliant mechanism of deposit protection in respect of Islamic Banking Institutions (IBIs), has been duly approved by State Bank’s Shariah Advisory Committee. Deposit Protection Waqf (DP Waqf) The objective of the Waqf (DP Waqf) is to provide a Shariah-compliant mechanism for protected depositors of IBIs up to an amount prescribed under Section 7 (1) of the Act. A DP Waqf is established under this mechanism for protection of depositors of IBIs. The nature of DP Waqf is of an account established by Corporation (DPC) to receive premium contributions from participatory/ member IBIs for the purpose of reimbursement of protected deposits of a failed participatory / member IBI. The DP Waqf shall be represented by the Corporation (DPC) in all its affairs. id: Foreign Currency Debit Card | FCY | Card Banking | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Meezan Foreign Currency (FCY) Debit Card for Exporters’ Special Foreign Currency Accounts (ESFCAs) In today’s interconnected world, we recognize the significance of seamless and convenient banking. That is why we are pleased to offer the Currency (FCY) Debit Card, which facilitates Pakistani exporters and freelancers holding an ESFCA account by providing unmatched global access to perform swift financial transactions. Freelancers and exporters can freely utilize Debit Card for making all types of current account payments abroad to meet their business needs, without prior approval from the SBP. These include but are not limited to the following: Payment for imports by exporters for the purpose of their own business. Payment for the acquisition of services/IT or digital services abroad by freelancers and IT exporters. Payments for dividends from domestic entities to their shareholders abroad, provided the shares owned are registered as 'Repatriable' with SBP. Operational expenses, including employee salaries, for liaison/marketing/representative offices abroad of local companies. Key Features & Benefits Worldwide Acceptance: The (FCY) Debit Card is powered by Mastercard and accepted at millions of outlets, online stores and ATMs worldwide*. *Cash withdrawals are restricted within Pakistan on Meezan FCY Debit Card. Spending Limits: (FCY) Debit Card meets your spending needs and provides access to your money with usage limits, including a daily limit of USD 5,000 for shopping and USD 500 for cash withdrawals. Enhanced Security: Debit Card is embedded with an EMV microchip, making it impossible for fraudsters to duplicate or clone your card. The card also features 3D Secure, adding an additional layer of security for e-commerce transactions. NFC Tap & Go: Debit Card is NFC (Near Field Communication) enabled, providing you the convenience of making contactless payments worldwide at retail outlets. *Fees, Charges & Transaction Limits To apply for Meezan FCY Debit Card, please visit your nearest branch. Issuance and Annual Charges FCY Debit Card As per SOC Card Replacement Charges (Lost, Stolen, Damaged) As per SOC Transactional Charges Cash Withdrawal (International) As per SOC International Purchase & Online Shopping As per SOC Transaction Limits (in USD) Transaction Type Single Transaction Limit Per Day Limit Cash Withdrawal (International) 500/- 500/- & Online Shopping (International & Domestic) 5,000/- 5,000/- *Note: The Bank may discontinue/suspend the FCY Debit Card services due to non-payment/non-recovery of associated charges. All charges are exclusive of applicable Government levies, including FED, provincial sales tax on services, and any other taxes. Conversion rate on international transactions will either be inter-bank rate or open market rate as per the prevailing regulatory instructions enforced. All international charges are settled in USD. FAQs Who can avail the Meezan FCY Debit Card? Meezan Bank customers having Special Foreign Currency Account (ESFCA) with the Bank can avail FCY Debit Card. This facility is exclusively available for freelancers and exporters. What transactions can be performed through my Debit Card? Meezan FCY Debit Cardholders

id: Foreign Currency Debit Card | FCY | Card Banking | Meezan Bank_chunk_1
text: in USD. FAQs Who can avail the Meezan FCY Debit Card? Meezan Bank customers having Special Foreign Currency Account (ESFCA) with the Bank can avail FCY Debit Card. This facility is exclusively available for freelancers and exporters. What transactions can be performed through my Debit Card? Meezan FCY Debit Cardholders can make all POS and e-commerce transactions abroad, along with ATM cash withdrawals. However, local ATM cash withdrawals are not permissible. How do I activate my Debit Card? Individual & Sole Proprietorship Account: To activate your debit card, call our 24/7 Call Centre at (+92 21) 111-331-331 or 111-331-332. The phone banking officer will activate your card upon successful verification. Entity Account (Partnership, Limited Companies / Corporations etc.): Within two working days, our Call Centre agent will contact the cardholder on the registered contact number provided in the issuance request and activate the card after successful verification, per the prevailing procedure. For how long is my card valid? The validity period of the card is five years from the date of issuance. The card expiry is mentioned on the front of the card. How do I change my ATM PIN? You can generate your ATM PIN by calling Call Centre at 111-331-331 or 111-331-332 (from outside Pakistan, call +92-21 111-331-331 or 111-331-332) and follow the directions through IVR (Interactive Voice Response). You may also change your ATM PIN any time by visiting the nearest Meezan Bank ATM. What should I do if my card gets stolen or lost? If you become aware that your debit card is lost or stolen, immediately at 111-331-331 or 111-331-332. From outside Pakistan, call +92-21-111-331-331 or +92-21-111-331-332 to report the loss or theft. You are protected from financial liability arising from any unauthorized transactions made on your card after it has been reported lost or stolen. Your old card will be cancelled, and a new card will be issued. Downloads Schedule of Charges Guidelines for ATM users Terms and Conditions MeezanGPT Assalam o Alaikum! Alhamdulillah, Meezan Investments has been operating as a full-fledged Shariah Compliant Asset Management Company since 1995. As the largest fund manager in Pakistan, we proudly manage over Rs. 701 Billion* in Assets Under Management, serving a diverse customer base of over 554,000* investors. We also hold the highest management quality rating of AM1, awarded by both VIS and PACRA. We offer a comprehensive range of Shariah Compliant investment solutions, including Mutual Funds, Pension Funds, and investment advisory services. id: Meezan Visa Infinite Debit Card | Meezan Bank_chunk_3
text: ATMs located at its branches and prominent offsite locations. There is no need to carry cash anymore; simply go to the ATM and withdraw cash anywhere, anytime. We also offer access to more than 8,000 ATMs country-wide via 1Link and MNET Networks. FAQs What is a Visa Debit Card Visa Debit Card is an internationally accepted Visa ATM/Debit Card that is linked to your Meezan Bank account, allowing spending on shopping outlets and cash withdrawals at ATMs. What is the difference between a debit and credit card? Visa Debit Card allows you to pay for your purchases using your own money through the Visa network, while a credit card provides you with the ability to access credit, which you then repay at a later date. This means with Visa Debit Card you can access the Visa network your own money without worrying about receiving a credit card bill at the end of each month. How do I make a purchase with my debit card? On your purchase the cashier will ‘swipe’ the card and give you a charge slip. Check the transaction amount and sign the slip. Once the transaction is complete, the amount will be deducted from your Meezan Bank account. For how long is my card valid? The validity period of the card is 5 years from the date of issue. The card expiry month is mentioned on the front of the card. How do I change my ATM pin? You can generate your ATM PIN by calling up our 24/7 call center on 111-331-331 or 111-331-332 (from outside Pakistan call +92-21 111-331-331 or 111-331-332) and follow the directions through IVR (interactive voice response). You may also change your ATM PIN any time by visiting the nearest Meezan Bank ATM. What should I do if my card gets stolen or lost? If you become aware that your debit card is lost or stolen, immediately call 111-331-331 or 111-331-332) to report loss or theft. You are protected from financial liability arising from any unauthorised transaction done on your card after being reported lost/stolen. We will cancel your old card and order a new one for you straight away. Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Articles & Reports Insights into Islamic Banking & Finance Islamic Banking: Case Study Prudence of Islamic Banks SBP Strategic Plan KAP Study Evolution of Islamic Banking Futures, Options & Swaps Shariah Investment Funds Challenge for the Ummah Judgement on Riba Islamic Securitization Microfinance Sukuk Launch of IIBR IB Industry Growth Islamic Banking: A Case Study of Meezan Bank PDF - 2013 Islamic banks are playing an active role in this aspect by putting comprehensive Shariah controls and mechanisms in place. The Pakistani Islamic Banking industry, particularly, Meezan Bank has made notable efforts and progress in this area. In the year 2013, Meezan Bank was recognized as the ‘Best Research & Development’ institution by the Global Islamic Finance Awards. This achievement goes onto show the support of the central bank which has led Meezan Bank as being referred to, a benchmark of Shariah Compliance in both local & global Islamic Finance industry. Download PDF Examining the Prudence of Islamic Banks: A Risk Management Perspective PDF - Risk Management Riba (interest) free and Gharar (uncertainty) free nature of Islamic banking together with real asset/service-backed transactions ensure that an efficient, effective and robust risk management mechanism is in place in Islamic banks. Download PDF SBP Strategic Plan for Islamic Banking PDF - Policy Insight The recent re-launch of Islamic Banking in Pakistan by SBP has been based not only on the lessons learnt from the history of Islamic Banking efforts in Pakistan but also on the experiences of other countries in the world that are currently known for their lead role in Islamic finance sector e.g.; Malaysia and Bahrain. Download PDF KAP Study: Knowledge, Attitude and Practices Banking in Pakistan PDF - Survey Analysis This initiative is a reflection of State Bank of Pakistan’s (SBP) commitment to promote Islamic banking in the country. The project owes to UK Department for International Development (DFID) for financing this study. Download KAP Study Download Graph Summary of Banking PDF - Historical Perspective The Governor of the State Bank of Pakistan, Dr. Ishrat Husain gave a comprehensive outline on the evolution Islamic Banking in Pakistan. He presented his paper at a seminar on Islamic Banking held recently at the Islamic Chamber of Commerce & Industry building, Karachi sponsored by Meezan Bank Limited. According to one view derivatives add value and are justifiable on efficiency grounds. At the same time, the Shariah does not perhaps explicitly forbid such contracts, as these are of recent origin. id: Meezan Women Entrepreneurs | Women’s Banking | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Women First Account Women First Consumer Finance Women Entrepreneurs If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Meezan Women First – Banking for Women the Right Way! Meezan Women First is a part of State Bank of Pakistan’s Banking on Equality initiatives that aims to reduce the gender gap by enhancing women’s financial inclusion To improve women’s access to financial products and services and to help them transform their lives by providing equal access to affordable Islamic financial services, Meezan Bank is offering an exclusively designed holistic products suit under the umbrella “Meezan Women First”. In addition to liability services, the Bank is also providing asset financing specifically tailored for women which includes SME, Commercial and Agriculture financing. As a Bank, we are committed to supporting women entrepreneurs in their business ventures. Islamic SME Asaan Finance (I-SAAF) Scheme Infrastructure, Housing & SME Finance Department In line with the vision of the Government of Pakistan, the State Bank of Pakistan launched a financing scheme called SME Asaan Finance (I-SAAF), specifically targeted at the growth of small businesses. This scheme provides SMEs with financing up to PKR 10 million at a discounted rate of 9% per annum. Financing is available only to companies/customers who are not existing borrowers of Meezan Bank. It may be availed for short-term working capital needs or long-term financing for the purchase of commercial equipment, machinery, vehicles, or property. I-SAAF Salient Features Eligibility All SMEs (new or existing businesses) not having a current relationship with Meezan Bank are eligible. The facility can be availed from one bank only. Types of Financing Term finance and working capital lines. Financing Limit Up PKR 10 million Pricing 9% per annum Tenor As per the approved terms and conditions of the facility. Security Collateral/security as per the Bank’s internal policy. *Other terms and conditions apply as per Meezan Bank’s and SBP’s policies from time to time. Prime Minister Youth Business & Agri Financing Scheme To provide self-employment opportunities for women, the Government of Pakistan has introduced financing scheme under the banner of Agri Financing Scheme (PMYB & AFS) offering subsidized financings to meet long-term and short-term financing needs. Key Customer Eligibility Criteria All female citizens of Pakistan holding CNIC, aged between 21 and 45 years with entrepreneurial potential. For IT/E-Commerce related businesses, the lower age limit will be 18 years with at least matriculation or equivalent education. Age limit relaxation is applicable on individuals and sole proprietors. In case of all other forms of business including partnerships and companies, only one of the owners, partners or directors must be in the age bracket prescribed above. Small and Medium Enterprises (start-ups and existing businesses) owned by youth as per above mentioned age brackets are also eligible. In case of agriculture, farmers’ classification as per SBP’s “Indicative Credit Limits & Eligible Items for Agriculture Financing 2020” will be applicable. Financing under the scheme should

id: Smart Asaan Mobile Account | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Asaan Mobile Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Open your account even from your feature phone and earn halal profit on your savings! Asaan Mobile Account is a digital wallet that enables banked as well as unbanked individuals to perform financial transactions securely even from their basic mobile phone. With faster and more convenient service, the Mobile Account is designed to allow individuals to send or receive money easily to any account within Pakistan without internet connectivity. Asaan Mobile Account also offers a saving facility that gives competitive profit rate on savings along with the flexibility to use the money at any time. Requirements for Using Meezan Asaan Mobile Account Asaan Mobile Account can be opened in PKR only. A valid CNIC is required for opening this account. Mobile Account can only be activated after successful verification. Key Features Instant account opening Instant account opening facility Link channel Facility to link existing smart wallet with AMA channel Biometric scanning Cash withdrawal facility from any Meezan Bank branch after biometric verification Transaction execution from basic mobile phone No Internet Required No internet facility required to access account Internet and Online Banking Free internet and mobile banking services Deposit No minimum deposit requirement Profit paid on monthly basis and calculated on daily basis Terms & Conditions Key Fact Statements (KFS) How Asaan Mobile Account Works? Download Fatwa On agreeing to become a Asaan Mobile Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool and fund from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharkah. Profit Sharing Ratio Bank will share 50% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 Products Tier Groups Weightage Assigned Asaan Mobile Account Monthly 0.46 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Tenure Profit Mobile Account Monthly 6.34% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates How to use Smart Asaan Mobile Account? Simply dial the short code *2262# in order to avail this service. MeezanGPT Assalam o Alaikum! How can I be of service to you today? FAQs What is a Visa Debit Card is an internationally accepted Visa ATM/Debit Card that is linked to your Meezan Bank account, allowing spending on shopping outlets and cash withdrawals at ATMs. What is the difference between a debit and credit card? Visa Debit Card allows you to pay for your purchases using your own money through the Visa network, while a credit card provides you with the ability to access credit, which you then repay at a later date. This means with Visa Debit Card you can access the Visa network your own money without worrying about receiving a credit card bill at the end of each month. How do I make a purchase with my debit card? On your purchase the cashier will ‘swipe’ the card and give you a charge slip. Check the transaction amount and sign the slip. id: Visa Debit Card | Debit Cards | Card Banking | Meezan Bank_chunk_2
text: make a purchase with my debit card? On your purchase the cashier will ‘swipe’ the card and give you a charge slip. Check the transaction amount and sign the slip. Once the transaction is complete, the amount will be deducted from your Meezan Bank account. For how long is my card valid? The validity period of the card is 5 years from the date of issue. The card expiry month is mentioned on the front of the card. What is the maximum amount that I can withdraw using my Visa Debit Card? Silver Card - ATM Cash Withdrawal (local) Rs. 50,000 per day ATM Cash Withdrawal (International) equivalent of per day - POS Purchase & E-Commerce limit (local) Rs. 100,000 & E-Commerce limit equivalent of per day - NFC Tap & Pay 100,000 per day and Rs. 5,000 per transaction & Pay 5,000 per transaction Gold Withdrawal limit (local) Rs. 150,000 equivalent of 150,000 5,000 per transaction How do I change my ATM pin? You can generate your ATM PIN by calling up our 24/7 call center on 111-331-331 or 111-331-332 (from outside Pakistan call +92-21 111-331-331 or 111-331-332) and follow the directions through IVR (interactive voice response). You may also change your ATM PIN any time by visiting the nearest Meezan Bank ATM. What should I do if my card gets stolen or lost? If you become aware that your debit card is lost or stolen, immediately call 111-331-331 or 111-331-332) to report loss or theft. You are protected from financial liability arising from any unauthorised transaction done on your card after being reported lost/stolen. We will cancel your old card and order a new one for you straight away. Can I use my Meezan Debit Card for online purchases / e-commerce transactions? Yes, use your Meezan Debit Card pay for your online purchases. Meezan Bank Visa and Mastercard Debit Cards are EMV 3-D Secure and you will be required to provide 'One Time Password (OTP)' received on your registered email/mobile number when making a payment against purchasing from a 3-D Secure merchant. Meezan Bank, as issuer bank requires all merchants to generate OTP for verification purposes, however merchants are free to bypass OTP as per their own discretion, which is allowed by international payment schemes. In the event where no OTP is generated by the merchant and the transaction is disputed by the customer, it will be dealt with according to prevailing 3-D secure rules specified by respective international payment schemes. Note: Please never share your card credentials such as you Card Number, Expiry, CVV & One Time Password (OTP) with anyone. Meezan Bank will not be liable for any unauthorized use of your card. Do I have to activate my card for e-commerce transactions? All Meezan Debit Cards are activated by default for all local and international e-commerce transactions. I have to enter PIN for every transaction? There will be instances where customer is not prompted to enter a PIN when making a purchase for the convenience

To achieve this objective, the Bank strives to provide consistent delivery of its products and services within the highest service parameters while ensuring fair treatment of customers at all stages of banking relationship. Meezan Bank developed a framework of Consumer Financial Protection which comprises of guiding principles for the areas such as Business Planning, Sales, Product Development, Customer Services, Financial Education and Awareness, Complaint Handling so that its customers make informed financial decisions, understand and exercise their rights and have effective recourse for their grievances. With the implementation Consumer Financial Protection framework, Meezan Bank intends to ensure the following: Customers are treated fairly of banking relationship. He is also a qualified Certified Shariah Advisor & Auditor CSAA from Accounting and Auditing Organization for Islamic Financial Institution (AAOIFI). Mufti Naveed Alam joined Meezan Bank in 2013 as a member of the Shariah Compliance Unit of Shariah Compliance Department. His main responsibilities include Islamic Banking training, Shariah Compliance Review and Shariah Audit of different Departments and Branches. He is an active Teacher/Trainer at Center for Excellence in Islamic Finance (CEIF) of Institute of Business Administration (IBA) Karachi, Center of Islamic Economics (CIE) of Jamia Darul-Uloom Karachi as well as several other renowned universities. Previously, Mufti Naveed Alam has served as Shariah Advisor of Premier Window Takaful Operations and also as Shariah Coordinator at Indus Hospital. is also of the Shariah Board of NBP Funds. Dr. Muhammad Imran Ashraf Usmani Shariah Board Member Dr. Muhammad Imran Usmani, son of Justice (Retd.) Mufti Muhammad Taqi Usmani, holds an LLB, M. Phil, and Ph. D. in Islamic Finance and graduated as a scholar with specialization (Takhassus) in Islamic Fiqh and fatwa from Jamia Darul-Uloom, Karachi. Muhammad Imran Usmani, Shariah Advisor and Group Head of the Product Development & Shariah Compliance Department (PDSC) at Meezan Bank, is responsible for R&D of Islamic banking products, advisory for Shariah-compliant banking and supervision of Shariah Audit & Compliance. Dr. Imran Usmani has served as an advisor / member of Shariah Boards of several renowned institutions since 1997 which includes the State Bank of Pakistan, HSBC - Amanah Finance, UBS - Switzerland, Guidance Financial Group USA, Lloyds TSB Bank - UK, Japan Bank for International Cooperation (JABIC), Credit Suisse Switzerland, RBS Global, Future Growth Albarakah Equity Fund South Africa, AIG Takaful, ACR Retakaful Malaysia, Capitas Group USA, Bank of London And Middle East Kuwait, BMI Bank Bahrain, Al Khaliji Bank Qatar, Sarasin Bank Switzerland, DCD group Dubai and other Mutual and Property funds and international Sukuk etc. He is also an Executive Committee Member of AAOIFI (Dubai) and Shariah Supervisory Board of International Islamic Financial Market (IIFM) Bahrain and International Center for Education in Islamic Finance (INCEIF) Malaysia, of Business Administration (IBA), Karachi and Center for Islamic Economics (CIE), Karachi. Dr. Imran Usmani is the author of numerous publications related to Islamic finance and other Shariah-related subjects. He has presented papers in numerous national and international seminars and has delivered lectures at academic institutions including Harvard, LSE, LUMS and IBA. Dr. Usmani is also the head of Hira Foundation School, a project of Jamia Darul Uloom Karachi to provide a high standard education from Montessori level to University levels combining conventional modern academics with Islamic teachings. id: Organizational Chart | Meezan Bank_chunk_1
text: has delivered lectures at academic institutions including Harvard, LSE, LUMS and IBA. Dr. Usmani is also the head of Hira Foundation School, a project of Jamia Darul Uloom Karachi to provide a high standard education from Montessori level to University levels combining conventional modern academics with Islamic teachings. He also serves as a lecturer of different subjects of Shariah and is amongst the administration board of the Jamia Darul-Uloom, Karachi. He is a visiting faculty member at Karachi University and IBA. He has also been leading Friday khuthbah and prayer at a Jamia Mosque for 20 years. Mr. Ahmed Ali Siddiqui Group Head Consumer Finance Ahmed Ali Siddiqui serves as Head Consumer Finance at Meezan Bank. He holds Bachelor’s & Master’s degrees in Business Administration from the Administration (IBA), Karachi with six gold medals. Mr. Siddiqui has over 23 years of Islamic banking experience and has been involved in pioneering Islamic finance initiatives in Pakistan, with expertise in Shariah-compliant product development, sovereign Sukuk structuring, banking solutions, policy, training, Shariah audit, research, and advisory. Before assuming his current role, Mr. Siddiqui served as a Secretary to the Shariah Supervisory Board and as Group Head Shariah Compliance at Meezan Bank, where he led one of the world’s largest Islamic banking research functions. He is also the Founding Director of Business Administration Centre in Islamic Finance (IBA CEIF), a global hub for Islamic finance research, education, and training. Mr. Siddiqui is part of the Joint Financial Advisors team to the Government of Pakistan for the issuance of local currency sovereign Sukuk and other Islamic financing transactions. He also led the consortium of Islamic banks in Pakistan for the Pakistan Energy Sukuk I & II issued by the Ministry of Energy, Government of Pakistan, and led the team the issuance of Pakistan’s first listed Sovereign Green Sukuk. Mr. Siddiqui has served on and is a member of State Bank-level task force on Islamic banking products, regulations, governance, accounting disclosures, and the adoption of for Islamic Financial Institutions (AAOIFI) Shariah Standards. He was named in the Islamica 500 in 2017 and 2019, and is a Founding Trustee of Ihsan Trust, where he served as Managing Trustee until 2022. Mr. Irfan Siddiqui Founding President & CEO Mr. Irfan Siddiqui is the founding President and CEO of Meezan Bank since 1997 to date. He is also member of Information Technology Committee and IFRS 9 Committee of the Board. Having articled with Coopers & Lybrand, London from 1975 - 1979, Mr. Siddiqui qualified as a Chartered Accountant the Institute of Chartered Accountants in England and Wales. His distinguished career includes several senior management roles, such as Chief Executive Officer at Al Meezan Investment Bank Limited, General Manager at Pakistan Kuwait Investment Company, Chairman of Al Meezan Investment Management, Advisor to the Managing Director at Kuwait Investment Authority, Manager of Finance and Operations at Abu Dhabi Investment Company, and Senior Business Analyst at Exxon Chemical (Pakistan) Ltd. Current Directorships: Member of Board of Trustees - Islamic Financial Institutions (AAOIFI),

text: introduction: میزان بینک پاکستان کا سب سے بڑا اسلامی بینک ہے، جو ملک بھر کے 320 سے زائد شہروں میں 1000 سے زائد برانچوں کے ساتھ کام کر رہا ہے۔ بینک کو مختلف مقامی اور بین الاقوامی اداروں نے کئی مواقع پر اس کی غیر معمولی سال بہ سال ترقی اور خطے میں اسلامک فنانس کے شعبے میں شراکت کے لیے تسلیم کیا ہے۔ میزان بینک کو پاکستان بینکنگ ایوارڈز – 2023 میں تیسری بار پاکستان کے بہترین بینک کے طور پر تسلیم کیا گیا ہے، جو ملک کے مالیاتی شعبے کا سب سے باوقار ایوارڈ ہے۔ ایوارڈ کی تقریب انسٹی ٹیوٹ آف بینکرز پاکستان نے ڈان میڈیا گروپ کے اشتراک اور اے ایف فرگوسن اینڈ کمپنی کے اشتراک سے منعقد کی ہے۔ ججنگ پینل، جو کہ مالیاتی شعبے میں کافی تجربہ رکھنے والے معزز شخصیات پر مشتمل تھا، اس کی صدارت اسٹیٹ بینک آف پاکستان (SBP) کے سابق گورنر جناب سید سلیم رضا نے کی۔ پریمیئر اسلامی بینک – میزان بینک، پاکستان کا پہلا اور بڑا اسلامی بینک جو آپ کی مالی ضروریات کو پورا کرنے کے لیے شریعت کے مطابق مصنوعات اور خدمات کی ایک رینج پیش کرتا ہے۔ بینک اسلامی شریعت کے اصولوں کے تحت سختی سے کام کرتا ہے اور اپنی مصنوعات کی ترقی کی صلاحیت، اسلامی بینکاری تحقیق اور مشاورتی خدمات کے لیے معروف ہے۔ کار ایجارہ میزان بینک کی کار فنانسنگ پروڈکٹ ہے اور یہ پاکستان کی پہلی بلاسود کار فنانسنگ ہے۔ یہ اجارہ (لیزنگ) کے اسلامی فنانسنگ موڈ پر مبنی ہے۔ یہ پروڈکٹ ان افراد کے لیے مثالی ہے جو کار کے حصول کے لیے بلا سود فنانسنگ حاصل کرنا چاہتے ہیں۔ ایزی ہوم – اسلامک ہاؤس فنانس آپ کے گھر کی مالی ضروریات کے لیے مکمل طور پر سود سے پاک حل ہے۔ روایتی ہاؤس لون کے برعکس، میزان بینک کا ایزی ہوم کم ہوتے مشارکہ کے ذریعے کام کرتا ہے جہاں آپ میزان بینک کے ساتھ اپنی جائیداد کی مشترکہ ملکیت میں حصہ لیتے ہیں۔ بائیک اجارہ پاکستان کی پہلی ربا سے پاک موٹرسائیکل فنانسنگ سہولت ہے جو پر مبنی ہے۔ فری لانسرز اب میزان بینک میں اکاؤنٹ کھول سکتے ہیں!
