id: Meezan Kafalah | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Meezan Kafalah Give your Dreams the Protection of Savings and Takaful If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print What is Meezan Kafalah? Meezan Kafalah is a Takaful (Islamic Insurance) plan through which customers can save for their future plans such as education or wedding of their child, going for Hajj, planning for old age etc. Kafalah is a Shariah-compliant alternative to bancassurance with easy exit option at any time. Saving, Investment & Protection Plans start as low as Rs.2,000/month Plans are available from 3 years to 15 years Flexible payment options: Monthly / Quarterly / Annually / Lump sum Continuous protection through Takaful Company Monthly profit payment reinvested in your Kafalah plan Easy exit option * No hidden charges on premature encashment Tailor-made options are also available Conveniently track your investment growth through complimentary internet banking Maximum protection of life Takaful up to Rs 15 Mln with additional 100% Accidental Death Benefits (ADB) throughout the plan term A Minimum coverage of Rs. 500, 000 upon Accidental Death Benefit (ADB) claim 100% cover for individuals with Permanent Total Disability (PTD) due to an accident or sickness. On Bank’s sole discretion, WAKALAH FEE may be waived after 3 or more years of WAKALAH period Sole Proprietorship & Joint Account Holder can also open Kafalah Account In case of Natural or Accidental Death Meezan Bank will also provide Additional Rupees 20,000 as Funeral Expense * 5% Wakalah fee and Takaful expense on actual rate applicable. Kafalah Products Higher Education Wedding Hajj Retirement How it Works Kafalah is a recurring deposit plan, where you, as a customer, will pay your contribution regularly The Bank will pay profit on your deposited amount while the Takaful partner will provide Takaful cover. Profit Sharing Ratio Bank will share 50% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 Products Tier Groups Weightage Assigned Meezan Kafalah Monthly 0.45 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Tenure Profit Meezan Kafalah Monthly 6.34% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates Early Redemption 100% cash value from day one alongwith daily profit.* Flexible exit option. Instant availability of funds. As Takaful cover is provided by Takaful provider, Takaful expense will be charged to customer at actual cost. *5% WAKALAH fee on first year permium Takaful expense on Takaful covered at actual basis. Claim Process Meezan Bank, acting as a facilitator, will forward the customer’s case to the Takaful company. The claim acceptance, processing and payment are sole responsibility of the Takaful company, and Meezan Bank shall not be liable for any grievances the customer may have with respect to Takaful coverage whatsoever. Accidental Death Benefit In case the individual covered suffers from accidental death the Takaful coverage amount of the Kafalah shall be double the coverage amount. metadata.source_title: Meezan Kafalah | Meezan Bank

id: Meezan Kafalah | Meezan Bank_chunk_1
text: for any grievances the customer may have with respect to Takaful coverage whatsoever. Accidental Death Benefit In case the individual covered suffers from accidental death the Takaful coverage amount of the Kafalah shall be double the coverage amount. Example Mr. Asad has subscribed a 10 year Kafalah plan for his son’s education. His annual contribution is Rs. 100,000/-. In case of natural death, his Takaful coverage shall be 10 years * Rs.100,000/- = 1,000,000/- & in case of the coverage shall be Rs.2,000,000/- Benefit In case of the customer’s death, a sum of the cash value (invested amount along with the profit earned) and the Takaful cover shall be paid. *The cash value will be paid by Meezan Bank to the legal heirs in accordance with the Bank’s deceased policy. *Takaful cover be paid by Takaful Company, as Hiba to the nominee. What is 100% Accidental Death Benefit (ADB)? Incase the customer's death is to an accident then 100% of life Takaful will be paid additionally as Accidentally Death Benefits (ADB). Eligibility Age All individuals in the age bracket of 18-55 years. Maximum age till maturity is up to 60 years. Initial Investment The initial investment required is Rs. 2,000/- only. Min. Investment Minimum investment is Rs. 24,000 per year or Rs. 2,000 per month How is Kafalah Shariah-compliant? Download Fatwa Meezan Kafalah works on the underlying contracts of Mudarabah and Wakalah. In Mudarabah, funds deposited by the customer are allocated to a deposit pool, which are invested strictly in Islamic modes of finance. In Wakalah, the customer will agree to appoint Meezan Bank as his/her Wakeel (agent) for facilitation in the collection of Takaful cover contribution, whereby the Bank may, acting as a collection agent, collect Takaful cover contribution i.e Takaful expense amount from the customer and change WAKALAH fee inlieu of providing Takaful coverage, which on years of WAKALAH period. text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Dollar Current Account Open a Dollar Current Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Key Features Free Chequebook No charges on issuance of chequebook Prior Notice Withdrawals of above $500 needs 1 day prior notice Minimum Deposit Minimum deposit required to open an account is $100 Foreign Remittances Receive Foreign Remittances Routine Withdrawals Routine withdrawals up to $500 per day allowed without prior intimation No Deductions No deduction of service charges if the balance maintained is low How the Dollar Account Works Download Fatwa Meezan Dollar Current Account is based on Qard contract where the Bank is liable to pay your money back on demand. The Bank can use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with an Islamic bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles. It is therefore an excellent business tool for individuals and corporate clients both for meeting their business needs and for diversifying their investment in different foreign currencies to safeguard against currency fluctuations. Want to Dollar Current Account Download, Print, Fill Form PDF version of our printed Account Opening Form is also available for download. You can print the form, fill your details and submit it along with the required documents at your preferred branch. id: Smart Wallet Savings | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Open Smart Wallet in no time and earn halal profit on your savings! Smart Wallet is a digital wallet that enables banked and unbanked population of the country to open accounts without the hassle of paperwork. Open Smart Wallet through simple biometric verification and enhance your savings by earning Halal monthly returns on savings, with the flexibility to use the money at any time. Requirements for Using Meezan Smart Wallet - Savings Smart Wallet can be opened in PKR Only customers having a valid CNIC can open this account Smart Wallet can only be opened after successful verification Pay Pak Debit Cards issued upon request on can only be used locally (Charges Apply) Key Features Instant account opening Instant account opening ATM access Nationwide ATM access through PayPak Debit card (If applied for) Biometric scanning Cash withdrawal from any Meezan branch after biometric verification Deposit No requirement of minimum deposit Internet and Online Banking Free internet and mobile banking services Profit will be paid on monthly basis and calculated on daily basis Terms & Conditions Key Fact Statements (KFS) How Wallet - Savings Works? Download Fatwa On agreeing to become a Smart Wallet Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized for Shariah compliant investment or to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharkah Profit Sharing Ratio Bank will share 50% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 Products Tier Groups Weightage Assigned Meezan Smart Wallet Monthly 0.46 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Amount Profit Smart Wallet Monthly 6.34% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates Want to Open a Wallet - Savings Visit a Branch You may visit any branch of Meezan Bank and our staff will assist you in opening your account. id: International Institute of Islamic Bankers | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login Welcome to Institute of Islamic Bankers! About IIIB Certificate Courses If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print of Islamic Bankers of Islamic Bankers (IIIB) in an Islamic Banking & Finance learning, development & awareness initiative of Meezan Bank Limited. The courses offered by IIIB are designed by experienced industry experts in a way that covers all major aspect of Islamic Finance. These courses majorly comprise of short real life case-studies that offer practical knowledge and strengthen Islamic Finance knowledge base with specialization in the specific areas of your interest in this field. Our leading course titled: ‘Certificate in Islamic Banking and Finance’ is renowned in the industry for the following reasons: Certified by Meezan Bank Unique case-study based pedagogy designed by current industry practitioners Short sessions with Shariah scholars & industry experts (on availability) Pakistan’s First Institute accredited by Finance Accreditation Agency Malaysia (FAA) Competitive pricing – especially for group registrations These factors collectively contribute to the overall authenticity of this certificate. Why Islamic Bankers (IIIB) Islamic Bankers (IIIB) the training and development wing of Meezan Bank Limited welcomes you to the world of Islamic Finance. It is a network of students, researchers, bankers and Islamic Finance professionals at a global level who learn and share the latest industry practices. IIIB offers various courses in Islamic Finance and recognizes the importance of practicality of the knowledge provided to its students. We constantly update the curriculum to ensure that our members and students are edified with up-to-date industry practices making them an edge over the competitors in industry and become trained Islamic Finance business leaders. Moreover, we are committed to upholding the highest ethical and professional standards and maintaining public confidence Islamic Banking and Finance. of Islamic Bankers is Finance Accreditation Agency (FAA) of Malaysia. FAA is an independent quality assurance and accreditation body supported by Bank Negara Malaysia (Central Bank of Malaysia) and Securities Commission Malaysia. id: Asaan Account |  Current Account | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Asaan Current Account Open an Asaan Current Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print For Customers with Limited Proof of Source of Income The Riba-free Meezan Asaan Current Account is another addition to the offerings of Meezan bank. Meezan Asaan Account opens the doors of banking products and services to those customers who have limited proof of source of income. The account can be opened with just Rs. 100/- and a valid CNIC/ SNIC / Passport / National Identity Card for Overseas Pakistanis (NICOP) / Pakistan Origin Card (POC) / Alien Registration Card (ARC) issued by National Aliens Registration Authority (NARA) / Pension Book. (Meezan Current Account is meant for resident individuals having Pakistani nationality only) Key Features No charges on Debit Card issuance: Customer can select any one card from VISA Classic, VISA Gold, Mastercard Classic, or PayPak Card (One free primary card per account) No charges on SMS Alerts subscription (1st time only) No charges on Cheque Book issuance Free Payorders, Free Online Banking, Free Internet Banking, Free E-Statements Facility, Free Digital Transaction Alert Facility (These services are also free on Meezan Bank Savings accounts). Minimum investment required to open an account is Rs. 100/- Maximum credit balance limit is Rs. 3,000,000/- Monthly debit limit of Rs. 1,000,000/- Disclaimer: Debit Card annual charges, replacement charges, Supplementary card charges will be as per prevailing Schedule of Charges After 1st time free SMS subscription, subsequent Schedule of Charges Why Are These Services* Not Charged? *(Debit Card, Sms Alerts & Cheque Book) Current Accounts operate on the principle of Qarz, with customers acting as lenders to the Bank. The above-mentioned services assist our customers in withdrawal of funds, essentially aiding the Bank in repaying its Qarz obligations, so they can be offered with or without any charges. Note: For further clarification, please refer to clause 10/1/3 of AAOIFI Shari’ah Standard No. 19 on Loan (Qard), adopted by SBP via IBD Circular No. 01 of 2020. How the Asaan Current Account Works Download Fatwa Current Account is based on Qard contract where the Bank is liable to pay the deposited funds back on demand. The Bank can use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles. Want to Open a Asaan Current Account? Download, Print, Fill Form PDF version of our printed Account Opening Form is also available for download. You can print the form, fill your details and submit it along with the required documents at your preferred branch. Download Form Visit a Branch You may visit any branch of Meezan Bank and our staff will assist you in filling the Account Opening Form and opening your account. text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Meezan eBiz + Customised Payments and Collections If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print A Scalable and Automatic Platform Meezan Bank’s eBiz+ (Payments & Cash Management Solution) is a scalable, automated platform optimized for organizations that consider secure and timely execution of their collections & payments a critical component of their business.Through eBiz+ Meezan Bank will enable its esteemed clientele to centralize their Collections & Payments efficiently while increasing visibility and control over their cash flows. Equipped with the most comprehensive array of service offerings, our system can manage the needs of businesses of all sizes while being modular in design for the greatest possible flexibility. Payments Centralize and simplify your payments In today’s competitive business environment management of a company’s working capital has become crucial as it helps in increasing liquidity while keeping costs low. Keeping this in mind Meezan Bank has engineered its comprehensive and fully optimized payments platform that minimizes costs and improves straight-through processing. Leveraging our comprehensive system you can make use of a wide assortment of payment methods both electronic and paper based. Impeccable integration With our advanced payment platform you can initiate transactions in singular or bulk form as well as gain host-to-host connectivity with your Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)systems. Complementing the payments module we provide you accessreal–time account information. This will aid you in maintaining a clear view of your organization's day-to-day cash positions, enabling you to make better use of your surplus balances. Digitize Your Authorization Schedule Replicate your company's signing authorities on our online environment by defining user permissions. Customizable as per the specific needs of your business.Gain increased control over your financial transactions through the ability to require multiple signing officers to approve electronic transactions. ebiz + Offers the Following Payment Related Services Internal Funds Transfer (Meezan to Meezan Account) eBiz+ portal can be used to transfer funds across any Meezan Bank Account across Pakistan on real time basis with straight through processing. Utility & Tax Payment (Meezan eBiz+ Application) Utility bills and tax related payment module is a recent addition in Meezan eBiz+ Application. Through this module customers will be able to manage their utility and tax related payments online 24/7 (within due dates only). Meezan Bank’s system is connected with 1link billing system for real time fetching of billing data to manage your payments smoothly and efficiently. Bills for utility companies available on the 1link can be paid online using Meezan eBiz+, subject to availability of the data of respective bill and that the bills are within due dates only. Meezan eBiz+ Application has provision to accommodate both single and bulk utility payments. eBiz+ Cheques (Meezan Bank Pay Order) eBiz+ Cheque (Pay Order) single or bulk issuance request can also be initiated using our eBiz+ module. The PO issuance request will land electronically at our designated CM processing center. The Payments & Cash Management team from there

The PO issuance request will land electronically at our designated CM processing center. The Payments & Cash Management team from there will issue the PO and will dispatch/deliver them as per customer instructions. Corporate Cheques (Cheque Outsourcing Services) Using our payment portal customers can request for issuance of Corporate Cheques single or bulk. Our designated CM processing Center will print the cheques with or without customer’s digital signatures as per the customer’s requirement and will be dispatched to the respective address based on the instructions provided. IBFT (Inter Bank Funds Transafer) IBFT will enable customers to transfer funds instantly between banks without any hassle via our payment platform. This service allows to transfer funds from their account to any of the millions of accounts of other 1Link member banks across the country. RTGS Request (Real Time Gross Settlement) & Cash Management System has a module which allows you to send RTGS requests as well. All such requests electronically at our central CM Operations system for onward processing. Security Meezan Bank’s Platform uses the highest level of security available in the market. All communications between your browser and the payment portal are fully encrypted using 256-bit SSL encryption on payment execution. It is optimized with a multi-factor authentication process for a more secured and fail safe transaction User ID / password Transaction PIN (daily / weekly / monthly) One Time Password Token Device (for Signatories only) Collections Streamlined Receivables Collections are critical to your business cycle. When it comes to receivables a company needs to provide as many avenues to its customers as possible. You need the ability to accept payments through multiple payment modes.Meezan Bank delivers the right array of collection services designed to meet the evolving needs of today's most complex organizations. Impeccable integration Meezan Bank Collection Services will aid in reducing your overall collection costs and maximize your working capital. Complementing the collections solution, we provide our customers with a transaction information portal from where you can access transactional information real-time from anywhere, at anytime. No matter what the business model our collections services can provide you assistance as you take your business to new levels of effectiveness, efficiency and profitability. Our dedicated team will enable you to: Realize collections nationwide Deposit all kinds of incoming instruments Receive online collections Automate your billing process Control & Predict your incoming cash flows Offers the Following Collections Related Services Customized Deposit Receipts CDS is a convenient way for the Corporate or Commercial Entity of going about their collection endeavor. metadata.source_title: Meezan EBiz Plus | Meezan Bank

id: Meezan EBiz Plus | Meezan Bank_chunk_2
text: Companies which have a decentralized control over their warehouses due to the remoteness of their locations. Leveraging Meezan Bank’s wide branch network dealers can deposits payments into the company account from any Meezan Bank branch. Payment Voucher This specific instrument is given to the distributors against their performed transactions. This securitized paper is a non-negotiable instrument which is proof that the Company account has been credited. The proposed product works in the interest of both the parties, providing the dealers the same benefits as a Pay Order while eliminating the ‘Transit Time’ for the customer whilst mitigating the risk of fraudulent activities. SIDA (Standing Instruction Debit Authority) Designed to create a single, convenient and efficient way of collections. This specific collection module concentrates all control in the hands of the customer. SIDA offers its customers increased control over cash flow while reducing the operational costs. SIDA can optimize the process of collecting payment obligations for non-micro enterprises. E-Collections Designed to cater the need of large companies which are centrally controlled and have a few large dealers handling their distribution. This dealer-initiated collection module allows stake holders of the customer to directly transfer funds to the company’s account by leveraging the power of the internet using our eBiz+ subsidiary portal access capability. The module significantly reduces the processing time of the transaction and maximizes your cash flow by immediately crediting your central account. Information Reporting Meezan Bank’s Information Reporting Platform provides users with an unprecedented level of visibility across all collections and disbursement activities. The reporting module hosts an array of specialized reports which can be customized as per the requirement of each client aiding you in managing and processing the financial information better. Leveraging an embedded reports engine, the module supports real-time access to transactional information and accountbalances. Apart from the MIS Reporting Capability the platform also houses a module whereby the cus-tomer can access the companies Account Statements, which can be downloaded into multiple formats Sweep Set-ups Based on the company instructions the surplus balance from your operating account is automatically swept into a savings account overnight. Funds are then transferred back and forth as per customer requirement. MIS Scheduler Transaction updates is an intra-day service which keeps you posted to the various debit and credit entries as they happen in your account at any given time of the day. With Interim Transaction Report you can stay on top of your cash position. Alert Services If you are an entity which keeps a constant watch on their cash flows then our alert engine is just what you need. Our alert service engine enables you to keep yourself updated of the incoming transactions down to the very second. Through this service you can proactively monitor your accounts and get instant updates wherever you are via both email and/or SMS. The alerts are modular and can be customized depending on the information you require to be present in it. For Queries and Assistance Call us at 021 - 36406139 PABX 021

text: service you can proactively monitor your accounts and get instant updates wherever you are via both email and/or SMS. For Queries and Assistance Call us at 021 - 36406139 PABX 021 - 38103500 Ext: 2318, 2224, 2316 support.cms@meezanbank.com Helpful Downloads : eBiz+ Brochure FAQs What facilities are available on Meezan eBiz+? Below is the list of facilities available on Meezan eBiz+: Issue Corporate Cheque Issue eBiz+ Cheques (Pay Order) Transfer Funds between Meezan Bank accounts (IFT) Request for RTGS Salary Disbursement Download Reports and Statements. View Account Balances and Statements. When can I use Meezan eBiz+? You can Log In to Meezan eBiz+ 24 hours a day, 7 days a week (refer to Terms & Conditions for details). How do I register for eBiz+? You can register for Meezan eBiz+ by filling out the Company and User Registration Forms along with the Terms & Conditions document. After successful registration and activation of internet banking account, you will be provided username, passwords and token (For Authorized Signatories Only) devices to operate Meezan eBiz+. What should I do when accessing for the first time? Once registered; you will receive an email from cmsalert@meezanbank.com bearing your User ID and Password. You can use these to login to our Meezan eBiz+ website. If you’re an Authorizer you will also require your Token device whilst logging in. Make sure that you have the required hardware and software. In order to maintain maximum security, when you login for the first time, you will be asked to change your Password. What if I forget my password or username or both? In case you forget your username, password or both, please email us on support.cms@meezanbank.com between banking hours for Password reset from your authorized Company email ID. should I do if I have a query which is not answered here? If you have any query(s) which is/are not covered here please email us at support.cms@meezanbank.com. How do I know that Meezan eBiz+ is secure? Meezan eBiz+ with its multi layered security architecture provides you with a safe and secure environment for carrying out your daily business transactions. The following security features ensure that only authorized users have access to their accounts: Secured Socket Layer (SSL) with 256 bit encryption to ensure complete privacy of data. 3-FA (Factor Authentication) i.e. three levels of authentication in the form of Password, PIN generated and Security Token Device. Time-Out feature which automatically terminates a session after a pre-defined period of inactivity. Auto account lock on three unsuccessful login attempts. What is Encryption? What level of Encryption do you use? Encryption allows information to travel between you and Meezan Bank in a secure environment that cannot be read by anyone else. It is a way of 'scrambling' information to make it impossible to read unless the recipient has the ability to 'un-scramble' it. The strength of encryption can be measured by the number of

metadata.source_title: Meezan EBiz Plus | Meezan Bank

id: Meezan EBiz Plus | Meezan Bank_chunk_4
text: travel between you and Meezan Bank in a secure environment that cannot be read by anyone else. It is a way of 'scrambling' information to make it impossible to read unless the recipient has the ability to 'un-scramble' it. The strength of encryption can be measured by the number of 'bits' used in the 'scrambling' process. The latest web browsers use 256-bit encryption: the most secure form currently available. Meezan eBiz+ can only be accessed using 256-bit encryption. What is the Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)? Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) is an Internet security standard that ensures all information transmitted your browser and Meezan eBiz+ is encrypted. 256-bit SSL is supported by all major browsers. We recommend you use the latest 'supported' (i.e. not beta) manufacturer browser with any security patches. Can I access Meezan eBiz+ if I am behind a corporate firewall? Companies who operate a corporate firewall may not permit access to all sites. You should consult with your Local Area Network (LAN) administrator if you are unable to access Meezan eBiz+. What must I do if may have divulged my eBiz+ Security Details? If you are concerned that you may have divulged your security details please call us on the following numbers immediately. Tel: 02138103500 Ext: 2320; 2621; 3603; 2322. must I do to protect the company account details and how can I ensure that no one else has access to the account(s)? Access to your company’s account details are controlled via the login process. It is therefore essential that you do not divulge or write down your username or password or share the token device. Take great care when logging in. You must change your password in case of suspicion or you can contact us at support.cms@meezanbank.com (Queries will be solved during bank hours) if you suspect someone has seen your username or password. In case your email address has been hacked, please contact the following numbers immediately and get the Meezan eBiz+ service blocked as all correspondence is delivered via email. do if I receive an email asking for my confidential information? Meezan Bank will never send emails that ask for confidential information. If you receive an email requesting your Meezan eBiz+ security details, you should not respond. These are not genuine emails. If you receive such an email, please do not click on any link or provide any Internet Banking log on details. While we may send you emails from time to time, we never send emails inquiring about your Internet banking information. Please forward it to us at support.cms@meezanbank.com. What is the minimum computer specification for using Meezan eBiz+ For optimal use and best performance, we recommend the following computer system and software settings: Operating System: Microsoft Windows XP, Windows 7, Windows 8. Hardware Requirements: Pentium or equivalent processor and 32MB RAM. 16 bit high color graphics. A modem (the faster the better), Access to the Internet through an Internet Service Provider (ISP) or Local Area Network (LAN). System Browser: Microsoft Internet Explorer 10.x

To check or alter your current resolution, please refer to your PC user manual. Does Internet Banking use Cookies? eBiz+ uses temporary cookies to enable our site to work efficiently. Cookies are small pieces of data which we store within your browser to allow us to see where you are within the site. These are deleted at the end of your session. MeezanGPT Assalam o Alaikum! text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Governance Corporate Profile Governance Investor Relations Financial Information Media Centre Social Media Contact If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Board of Directors Shariah Board Management Team Board Committees Board Human Resources, Remuneration & Compensation Committee Mr. Riyadh S.A.A. Edrees (Chairman) Mr. Mohammad Abdul Aleem Mr. Irfan Siddiqui Ms. Nausheen Ahmad Mr. Abdulrazzaq T.A.M. Razooqi Terms of Reference: The & Compensation Committee (BHRR&CC) ensures that the Bank manages its human resources in light of the best practices of the industry. It reviews and recommends human resource management policies to the Board of Directors including but not limited to remuneration practices defined by the State Bank of Pakistan, the selection, evaluation, compensation (including retirement benefits), succession planning as well as the recommendation of structure of compensation package of Executive Directors, President & CEO, Key Executives and of any other employee(s) along with ensuring implementation of the same. The BHRR&CC also monitors the training activities, utilization and implementation of training & development budget and policy of the Bank. Furthermore, the Committee evaluates sustainability risks, ESG considerations, and their financial implications, submitting annual progress reports to the Board. Board Risk Management Committee Mr. Saad Ur Rahman Khan (Chairman) Mr. Bader H.A.M.A. Al-Rabiah Mr. Zine Elabidine Bachiri Reference: The Risk Management Committee (BRMC) is responsible for assessing the Bank's policies on all major risk categories including credit, market, liquidity and operational risk well as the adequacy of the risk management function of the Bank. The BRMC reviews adequacy of the Bank's capital in accordance with laid down rules and regulations as per Basel Accord. It also reviews the techniques developed and implemented to measure the Bank's risk exposure. Its responsibilities include evaluation of the risk profile and appetite of the Bank and ensuring that systems are in place for monitoring overall risk The BRMC reviews exception reports highlighting deviations from the approved policies as well as deliberates upon risk related reports including Shariah non-compliance and early warning signals of potential risks emerging from Bank's activities. Board Audit Committee Mohammad Abdul Aleem (Chairman) Mr. Irfan Siddiqui Ur Rahman Khan Mr. Tariq Mahmood Pasha Reference: The Board Audit Committee (BAC) is responsible for determining appropriate measures to safeguard the Bank's assets; reviewing quarterly, half-yearly and annual financial statements; reviewing management letters/assurance reports issued by external auditors and management's response thereto; reviewing and implementing the scope, extent and plan of internal audit/Shariah audit; reviewing the internal audit strategy; considering major findings, internal investigations of activities characterized by fraud, corruption and abuse of power, SBP inspection / supervisory assessment findings management's response thereto; ascertaining that the internal control systems are adequate and effective; determining compliance with relevant statutory requirements; addressing audit observations; monitoring compliance with best practices of corporate governance and overseeing the implementation of Internal Controls over Financial Reporting (ICFR) program across the Bank, reviewing the effectiveness of whistleblowing procedures; ensure the effectiveness of overall management

metadata.source_title: Governance | Meezan Bank

id: Governance | Meezan Bank_chunk_1
text: control systems are adequate and effective; determining compliance with relevant statutory requirements; addressing audit observations; monitoring compliance with best practices of corporate governance and overseeing the implementation of Internal Controls over Financial Reporting (ICFR) program across the Bank, reviewing the effectiveness of whistleblowing procedures; ensure the effectiveness of overall management of compliance and AML risk; and considering any other issues or matters as may be assigned by the Board of Directors. Board Information Technology Khan (Chairman) Mr. Riyadh S.A. A. Edrees H.A.M.A. Al-Rabiah Mr. Irfan Siddqui Reference: The Information Technology Committee (BITC) advises and reports to the Board on the status of technology activities and digital initiatives. It periodically reviews IT and digital strategies, as well as relevant policies, in light of major technological and regulatory developments, and makes recommendations Board of Directors. The BITC ensures that risk management strategies are designed and implemented to achieve resilience, enabling the Bank to respond effectively to large-scale disruptions, including cyber-attacks. The Committee receives periodic updates from the IT Steering Committee to monitor major technology-related projects and ensures that technology procurements align with the IT strategy. It also approves cloud-based outsourcing arrangements in accordance with the policy approved by the Board. If deemed necessary, the Committee seeks expert opinions from independent sources, with the quorum comprising any two members. Board IFRS 9 Edrees (Chairman) Mr. Faisal Fahad Al-Muzaini Tariq Mahmood Pasha of Reference: The Committee is responsible for reviewing and approving the Bank’s transition plan for IFRS9 implementation. It is required to review progress against the transition plan on a quarterly basis. The Committee is also responsible to ensure smooth implementation of IFRS9 within the timelines stipulated State Bank of Pakistan. Pattern of Shareholding As at December 31, 2024 Auditors M/s. A. F. text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print The account that gives you more! Experience a new level of banking with the Meezan Plus Account, designed to meet all your financial needs. This comprehensive, current remunerative account is based on the Islamic principle of Mudarabah and is ideal for Individuals, Sole Proprietorships, Partnerships, and Companies. Enjoy added benefits and a rewarding banking experience by maintaining a monthly average balance of Rs. 200,000 or more. With Meezan Plus, your banking goes beyond transactions - it becomes an elevated experience. Key Features & Benefits Free Debit Card upon Account Opening: Customer can select any one card from VISA Classic, VISA Gold, Mastercard Classic, or PayPak Card (One free primary card per account) Free Cheque Book, Free Payorders, Free Online Banking, Free Internet Banking, Free E-Statements Facility, Free Digital Transaction Alert Facility Profit will be calculated Daily Profit will be paid Monthly Standard Charges Apply (except for free Meezan Plus services) Zakat and Withholding Tax deducted as per regulations Minimum investment required for account opening is Rs. 1,000/- No Maximum investment limit No restriction on withdrawals or number of transactions Exclusive Perks Following waivers can be availed by Meezan Plus account holders who maintain Rs. 200,000 or above**: No annual fee on debit cards (Meezan PayPak Debit Card / Meezan VISA Silver Card / Meezan Mastercard Classic Card / Meezan VISA– Gold Debit Card) Free On-Demand Account Statements Free Intercity Clearing through NIFT No Charges on Stop Payment Requests Free SMS Alerts (all transactions) No Charges for Balance Maintenance Certificate / Account Maintenance Certificate No Charges on Payorder cancellation **System will check the balance of Rs. 200,000/- of the previous month only before applying the above charges. For example: If the annual fee of the card is applicable in the month of May, 2025, the system will check the average balance of April, 2025 only. In the first month of opening an account, customer will be eligible for the waiver of the above services if the account balance is at least Rs. 200,000/- at the time of the transaction. How Does the Meezan Plus Account Work? Upon agreeing to become a Meezan Plus Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal), and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the deposited funds. The Bank allocates these funds to a deposit pool; which is then utilized to provide financing to customers through Islamic modes, including but not limited to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna, and Diminishing Musharakah. id: Meezan Roshan Apna Ghar | RDA | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Easy Home Roshan Apna Ghar If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Roshan Apna Ghar (Self Investment) To facilitate Non-Resident Pakistanis (NRPs) with owning real estate in Pakistan, Roshan Apna Ghar enables NRPs with a Meezan Roshan Digital Account (RDA), allowing them to invest in any residential and commercial property in the country on repatriable basis. Some key features of the arrangement are: • All funds will be invested from Meezan Roshan Digital PKR Account. All payments (token money, down payment/advance, payments made in full, partial, tranches, etc.) must be made from RDA - PKR to fully repatriate the proceeds/disinvestments in future. • A customer can invest in his/her own name or jointly with eligible family members (which includes parents, brother, sister, wife, children—lineal ascendant and descendant only). • Facility for direct payments in favor of seller only. • Full repatriation of proceeds*. The Bank shall conduct proper due diligence and require the customer to submit documents as per the Bank’s internal processes and regulatory guidelines. *In case of disinvestment before three years, the customer can repatriate funds up to the lower amount of sale proceeds or principal investment amount in PKR on the date of the investment, while the differential amount, if any, and any profit/return earned from eligible investments made out of this amount may be repatriated after the expiry of three years from the date of investment. Disclaimer: A customer must successfully register their application with the Bank before making any payment partial, tranches, etc.) to the seller for the purchase of property. Roshan Apna Ghar (Bank Financing) “Get your family a home of their dreams in Pakistan through Roshan Apna Ghar.” Roshan Apna Ghar is a Riba-free solution to customers’ home financing needs, designed especially for RDA holders and Islamic Naya Pakistan Certificate (INPC) holders. It works on the Shariah-compliant concept of Diminishing Musharakah where the Bank and Customer become joint owners in property. The Bank leases its share of property to Customer against periodic rentals. The Customer also purchases Bank’s share of property over the tenure to ultimately become sole owner of the property. Types of Bank Financing: • Lien - For customers who are willing to keep their investment in INPC or RDA deposit funds as lien against their net financing amount as collateral. In this case, no mortgage will be created on the property. • Non-Lien customers who are availing financing facility without cash collateral to invest in Roshan Apna Ghar (MRAG). In this case, an equitable or token mortgage will be required. Key Features Invest directly from your Roshan Digital PKR account (fully digital processing). Easily convert between foreign currencies (USD, EUR, & GBP) and PKR. Maximum financing with attractive pricing options including variable and five-year fixed rates*. A low processing fee of only Rs. 4000 (excluding FED). Flexibility to make partial unit purchases with no early termination profit after first

id: Meezan Roshan Apna Ghar | RDA | Meezan Bank_chunk_1
text: account (fully digital processing). Easily convert between foreign currencies (USD, EUR, & GBP) and PKR. Maximum financing with attractive pricing options including variable and five-year fixed rates*. A low processing fee of only Rs. 4000 (excluding FED). Flexibility to make partial unit purchases with no early termination profit after first year. For termination within first year, 1% extra profit will be applicable. Optional life Takaful covering death (due to any cause) and accidental/natural disability. Property Takaful (covering the financing amount) absolutely FREE for customers. *Terms and conditions apply. Kindly see the Eligibility Criteria. Eligibility Criteria Citizenship Lien Based All Non-Resident RDA holders having valid NICOP & POC. Non-Lien having valid NICOP Product Category Buyer, Builder, Renovation Segment Salaried Individual Non-salaried individual (individuals earning via their business) Age (Applicant & Co-Applicant) Lien Based Salaried individual : 20 to 65 years. Non-salaried to 65 years. Non-Lien to 65 years. In both types of financing, the age of applicant must not exceed 60 years. However, it may be extended to 65 years, provided applicant justifies that he/ she can make payments even after retirement age. Co-applicant’s age must not exceed 65 years at the time of maturity, incase income of co-applicant is not clubbed. However, if co-applicant’s income is clubbed, then the age criteria same as applicant will be applicable. Financing Range & Tenure Financing Range (Buyer, Builder) Minimum Rs. 500,000/- Maximum No cap Financing Range (Renovation) Lien Based Maximum No cap Non-Lien Rs. 500,000/- Maximum Rs. 10 Million Financing Tenure 3 to 25 Years Co-Applicant Maximum Number of Co-Applicants A maximum of four co-applicants are allowed. Lien Based Co-applicant is not mandatory in this case. However, if opted, he/she will be an immediate family member like a spouse, parent, adult sibling and/or children. Further, applicant also has an option of appointing a “nominee” to carry out any assignment on his/her behalf, having no legal obligations on nominee. Non-Lien Based Co-applicant is mandatory and an immediate family member, sibling and/or children. At least one should be permanently living in Pakistan. Co-applicant (NRP) for Income Clubbing Lien Based 100% Co-applicant income will be clubbed. Non-Lien income will be clubbed in case of spouse. 75% in case of other than spouse. For the purpose of income clubbing, only a non-resident Pakistanis can be considered. Net Monthly Income Lien Based There will be no minimum income criteria. Non-Lien no minimum income criteria however, maximum 50% DBR will be kept while providing financing facility. Employment Tenure Lien Based Minimum overall employment / business tenure will be one year. Non-Lien tenure will be two years Creation of Mortgage Lien Based No requirement of mortgage - equitable or registered Non-Lien Based Equitable mortgage with token registered mortgage Physical Presence Lien Based Not required, as the property can be transferred in the name of the ex-parte as per the customer's request. The customer’s nominee may witness the transaction to ensure that the deed/sale deed is duly executed and registered in his/her name. The customer shall provide original last title deed

Strategic Alliance with Naya Nazimabad - Easy Home Off-Plan Financing Meezan Bank, Pakistan’s first and largest Islamic bank, introduces Easy Home for Naya Nazimabad Projects — a Shariah-compliant home financing facility offering up to 70% financing for under-construction apartments. This unique solution enables you to finance your future home through a convenient, hassle-free and 100% Halal structure, backed by affordable profit rates. Through our partnership with Naya Nazimabad – one of Karachi’s leading master-planned communities – you now have the opportunity to own a home in a modern, secure and well-developed neighborhood, supported by Meezan Bank’s trusted Islamic financing model. The development features thoughtfully designed 4-bedroom apartments with modern amenities, offering an elevated lifestyle within a vibrant gated community. This exclusive financing facility is currently available only for Naya Nazimabad – Globe Residency FL-5. For detailed project information, unit availability, and apartment features, please visit: Naya Nazimabad Apartments Note: In case any developer / builder intends to enlist their project with Meezan Bank, kindly contact the Bank at the following email id: info.rag@meezanbank.com Products Easy Buyer Choose it and get ready to move in! text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Meezan Bachat Account Open a Meezan Bachat Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print A Savings Account with Halal Profit Meezan Bachat Account provides halal monthly profit for low income and middle-income group of customers with the same convenience and services of a regular savings account. It offers varying profit weightages to different amount tiers. Meezan Bachat Account can be opened by Salaried or Self-employed, Housewives and Retired individuals. Key Features Chequebook Issuance of chequebook in accordance with the Schedule of Charges (SOCs) Free Pay Orders Pay Orders Free Internet Banking Free Internet Banking facility Low Minimium Investment Minimum deposit required to open an account is Rs. 50,000/- Free Online Banking Free Online Banking service at all Meezan Bank branches Profit Payment Profit payment is made to your Meezan Bank account on a monthly basis Profit Calculation Profit will be calculated on the Daily Product Basis Illustration Effective 1st September 2023, profit calculation is Meezan Bachat Account shall be done in following manner. Example: Tier 1 - Rs 1 to 499.99 K 14% Tier 2 - Rs 500 K & Above 11.5% Scenario: If a customer maintains closing balance of Rs. 700, 000 per day in Meezan Bachat Account, the applicable profit calculation shall be as follows: Profit Calculation Rs 499,999 x 14% / 365 = Rs 191.78 for 1 day Rs 200,001 x 11.5% 365 = Rs 63.01 for 1 day Total Profit (before the impact of taxation) for 1 day = Rs 254.79 (Rs 191.78+ Rs 63.01) Note: Prior to 1st September 2023, if customer's balance fell in a higher tier, then entire amount was assigned weightage of that respective tier. How the Bachat Savings Account Works Download Fatwa On agreeing to become Meezan Bachat Account holder, the customer enters into a relationship based on Mudarabah with Meezan Bank. Under this relationship, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the funds deposited by the customers. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; funds from the pool are utilized to provide financing to customers under Islamic modes that include, but are not restricted to Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharkah. Profit Sharing Ratio Bank will share 50% of Gross Income as Mudarib Depositor Gross Income as Rab-ul-Maal Weightages for the Month of April 2026 Products Tier Groups Weightage Assigned Meezan Bachat Account Re.1 - 499.99 K 0.45 Meezan Bachat Account K & Above 0.45 Consolidated Weightages Profit Rates Profit Rates the Month of February 2026 Products Amount Profit - 499.99 K 6.34% K & Above 6.34% Profit Rates Historical Profit Rates Want to Open a Bachat Savings Account Download, Print, Fill Form PDF version of our printed Account Opening Form is also available for download. We are Pakistan’s first and largest dedicated Islamic bank and one of the fastest growing financial institutions in the banking sector of the country. id: Export Financing Product | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Export Financing Solutions The State Bank of Pakistan is committed to supporting exporters by ensuring uninterrupted access to credit. Aligned with this vision, Meezan Bank offers a comprehensive range of Export Financing solutions designed to meet the diverse needs of our valued customers. The Bank’s Export Finance Schemes provide concessional, short-term financing to exporters of manufacturing goods and services — with a special focus on value-added products. Key Features Pre-shipment Financing Murabaha Musawammah Istisna Tijarah Salam Running Musharakah Post-shipment Financing Murabaha / Musawammah Deferred Tijarah Running Musharakah Alternative to Export Bill Discounting Bai Salam Tijarah Running Musharakah – Alternate Export Bill Discounting (EBD) Non-Funded Facilities for Exporters by Meezan Bank Hedging Facilities (Wa’ad) Murabaha Meezan Bank offers Murabaha Financing, a sale-based transaction, wherein the Bank procures goods from the market and resells them to the exporter on a deferred payment basis, with full disclosure of both the cost price and the agreed profit margin, to meet the exporter’s raw material procurement needs. Musawammah Financing is also a sale-based transaction, in which procures goods from market and sells a deferred payment basis at a mutually negotiated price, without any reference to the Bank’s cost price, material procurement needs. Istisna is a sale and agency-based mode of financing in which Meezan Bank finances the manufacturing of specific goods based against an export order or contract, thereby fulfilling the exporter’s working capital requirements. The price and detailed specifications of the commodity are mutually determined and agreed upon by both parties. Tijarah Financing sale and agency-based facility wherein exporters sell their finished goods to the Bank on a cash basis. This arrangement enables exporters to meet their working capital needs by converting credit sales into immediate cash, thereby providing Shariah-compliant liquidity support. Salam sale and agency-based facility, wherein the Bank pays the full purchase price in advance for specified homogeneous goods, and the exporter undertakes to deliver the goods at a mutually agreed future date. This arrangement provides the exporter with advance funds against deferred delivery. Running Musharakah Under Running Musharakah financing, Meezan Bank enters into a partnership with the exporter by contributing capital and sharing profits according to a pre-agreed ratio, while losses are borne strictly in proportion to the respective capital contributions. The Bank holds the export bill as collateral, and the proceeds thereof may be retained by the Bank as an advance share of profit under the Musharakah, subject to final adjustment against the business's financial statements according to the agreed profit-sharing ratio. Murabaha / Musawammah Meezan Bank offers Murabaha/Musawammah financing to exporters only against a genuine purchase requirement, by accepting export bills as collateral; financing is provided for the procurement of raw materials under the Murabaha/Musawammah structure. Deferred Tijarah Meezan Bank purchases finished goods inventory from deferred payment basis, while appointing the exporter as its agent to sell these goods to the ultimate

Hedging Facilities (Wa’ad) To hedge against foreign currency price fluctuations, Bank offers a Shariah-compliant hedging facility on the basis of Wa’ad, in which the customer (exporter) promises to sell the foreign currency (FCY) to the Bank against PKR upon the receipt of export proceeds in the future. Refinance Schemes by Bank of Pakistan Meezan Bank is offering refinance facilities under the schemes offered Bank of Pakistan to facilitate economic development of the country. Available modes of financing: Islamic Export Refinance Scheme (IERS) Rupee-based discounting facility under Refinance Scheme (IERS) Islamic Long Term Finance Facility (ILTFF) Islamic Finance Facility for Renewable Energy (IFRE) Refinance Scheme (IERS) Meezan Bank offers refinance facilities to meet the short-term financing needs of its customers. Designed to address the unique requirements of exporters, this facility is available in both Part-I and Part-II of the State Bank of Pakistan’s Export Refinance Scheme. refinance facilities to provide financing against export bills and export receivables. This product is tailored diverse needs of exporters and is available for both post-shipment & pre-shipment export receivables as per the Enhanced Scope Export Refinance Scheme. Finance Facility (ILTFF) In order to facilitate export growth, Meezan Bank offers the Islamic Long-Term Financing Facility under Bank of Pakistan’s long term facility at concessionary rates for export-oriented units. This facility provides local currency financing for the purchase of new plant and machinery, whether imported pr locally manufactured, and is structured under the Diminishing Musharakah mode of financing. Renewable Energy (IFRE) Meezan Bank, in line with Bank of Pakistan’s initiative, offers the long-term Islamic Financing Renewable Energy (IFRE) to support the energy sector of the country via

Financing under IFRE shall be available for power projects using alternative/renewable energy sources (solar, wind, hydro, bio-fuels, bagasse cogeneration and geothermal as fuel) under mode of financing. Eligibility Available to export-oriented projects with annual exports of at least USD 5 million or 50% of sales (whichever is lower). Eligible sectors under the scheme: Please note that as per IH&SMEFD Circular Letter No. 2 of 2020, dated January 28, 2020, the scope of ILTFF has been extended to all sectors permitted under the Export Policy Order issued by the Ministry of Commerce from time to time. Accordingly, the earlier instructions regarding sectoral eligibility criteria specified in IH&SMEFD Circular No. 1 of 2018, dated February 14, 2018, stand revised/withdrawn Financing Limit Maximum limit for a single export-oriented unit is Rs. 5 billion. id: Shariah Advisory Services | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Shariah Advisory Services Providing Tailor-made Islamic Finance Solutions to Institutions Around the World Advisory Services Shariah Screening Criteria If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Extensive Experience and Expertise In line with our vision of establishing Islamic Banking as banking of first choice, Meezan Bank believes in working for the larger benefit of the society. Meezan Bank has the unique combination of experts in areas like Islamic Law & Jurisprudence, Accounting, Auditing, Capital Markets, Corporate Finance and Islamic Finance. Our expertise, accumulated over the years, enables us to provide world class service and unmatched Shariah-compliant solutions to most sophisticated problems in a timely manner. We’re working to expand the market, not merely our market share. The continuous interaction with the academia has enabled us to produce a stream of expert practitioners-cum-trainers. Our trainings are not only backed by solid theoretical foundations, they are continuously updated based on real life experiences. Our case studies help learners gain hands-on experience of various aspects of Islamic finance. Objective and Strategy Offer innovative value-added products and services to our customers within the bounds of Shariah, while optimizing the stakeholders’ value through an organizational culture based on learning, fairness, respect for individual enterprise and performance. Shariah Advisory Services team assists in 6 ways 1. Islamic Banking Product Development Islamic Finance, though working in the space provided by the Islamic principles, is very open to creativity and innovation. Financial Engineering to develop Shariah-compliant alternatives to conventional financial products is an important task undertaken by the Shariah Advisory function. Research and development of new Islamic financial instruments is also a vital and ongoing activity of the function. Product Development requirements and requests received from our client organizations are also provided with assistance and technical support. 2. Islamic Capital Market Services Shariah Advisory Services (SAS) provides following services within Islamic Capital markets details for which are mentioned below: Sukuk Structuring As a leader in structured finance products, Meezan Bank has been involved in Sukuk Structuring, Shariah Advisory and Syndication/Consortium financing, lead-managing a number of new Shariah-compliant Sukuk issues and syndicated transactions with its corporate clients. SAS provides assistance in execution of Sukuk transactions and development of Sukuk structures based on client-specific requirements. Our multi-layered approach enables both commercial and Shariah considerations to be incorporated into proposed structures. Emphasis is placed on developing structures to achieve wide Shariah acceptance and marketability of Sukuk. Mutual Funds Mutual Funds are an important financial intermediary in developing markets where capital markets are volatile and investors do not have the expertise, savings and time to devote themselves to making a well-diversified profitable and Shariah-compliant portfolio. Islamic investment principles require persistent Shariah Compliance. Islamic Mutual Funds are therefore catering to this important need and we provide them with technical expertise in managing their portfolios in Shariah-compliant manner. 3. Shariah Compliant Corporate Restructuring Interest is a common pain in the economy domestically and internationally.

metadata.source_title: Shariah Advisory Services | Meezan Bank

id: Shariah Advisory Services | Meezan Bank_chunk_1
text: and Shariah-compliant portfolio. Islamic investment principles require persistent Shariah Compliance. Islamic Mutual Funds are therefore catering to this important need and we provide them with technical expertise in managing their portfolios in Shariah-compliant manner. 3. Shariah Compliant Corporate Restructuring Interest is a common pain in the economy domestically and internationally. Interest based financial system necessitates the use of riskier instruments which being highly speculative can disrupt an economy in downturns. Islamic financial principles propose Shariah-compliant yet highly robust alternatives for the financial needs of the economy. Meezan Bank has had the unique experience of converting conventional banks into an Islamic Bank. The Bank also provides Shariah Technical and Support Services to corporate clients to convert all operations of the clients into making them Shariah-compliant. 4. Preparation of Shariah Governance and Regulatory Frameworks Meezan Bank offers a great support to any institution interested in the formation of a reputable Shariah Board. This support includes the development of Shariah Governance Framework including the reference of world renowned Shariah Scholars, assistance in finalizing the Terms of Reference (TOR) of the Shariah Advisor / Shariah Board and assistance in holding the Shariah Supervisory Board Meetings. 5. Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Complete Banking Solutions Small & Medium Businesses Meezan Bank provides Shariah-compliant banking services such as financing, trade, and deposit-related services to small and medium-sized enterprises. We combine the expertise of in-house product specialists and Shariah scholars to provide Shariah-compliant financing solutions to meet the working capital finance, import finance, export re-finance, long-term finance, documentary credit requirements, and project financing needs of our customers. Interested in SME Financing - Apply Now SBP Policy for Promotion of SME Finance Under the National Financial Inclusion Strategy (NFIS) and strategic direction of the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP), the SME sector has been identified as one of the key priority areas. In line with this strategic direction, key benchmarks to be achieved by 2020 are: i) Increase SME share from the existing 8% of private sector credit to 17%, and ii) Increase the number of borrowers from 174,000 to 500,000. To achieve these objectives, a policy has been prepared for the promotion of SME finance (conventional and Islamic) in Pakistan. This policy is built on nine key pillars, which include: improving the regulatory framework, upscaling of microfinance banks, risk mitigation strategies, simplified procedures for SME financing, program-based lending and value chain financing, capacity building and awareness creation, handholding of SMEs, leveraging technology, and simplification of the taxation regime. Download Policy Islamic SME Asaan Finance (I-SAAF) Scheme Infrastructure, Housing & SME Finance Department In line with the vision of the Government of Pakistan, Bank of Pakistan launched a financing scheme called SME Asaan Finance (I-SAAF), specifically targeted at the growth of small businesses. This scheme provides SMEs with financing up to PKR 10 million at a discounted rate of 9% per annum. Financing is available only to companies/customers who are not existing borrowers of Meezan Bank. It may be availed for short-term working capital needs or long-term financing for the purchase of commercial equipment, machinery, vehicles, or property. I-SAAF Salient Features Eligibility All SMEs (new or existing businesses) not having a current relationship with Meezan Bank are eligible. The facility can be availed from one bank only. Types of Financing Term finance and working capital lines. Financing Limit Up PKR 10 million Pricing 9% per annum Tenor As per the approved terms and conditions of the facility. Security Collateral/security as per the Bank’s internal policy. *Other terms and conditions apply as per Meezan Bank’s and SBP’s policies from time to time. text: Long Term: Monthly / Quarterly / Semiannually Short Term: Mutually decided between customer and bank. Charity charged @ 20% per annum on each day of delay. Short Term Financing Needs (Working Capital Finance) Meezan Bank offers complete solutions for all working capital needs including procurement of raw materials, asset purchases for trading, and financing overhead expenses. A full range of Shariah-compliant products is available to meet these requirements. Murabaha Learn More Murabaha is a sale transaction where the seller (Bank) discloses cost and profit to the buyer. The Bank purchases the goods through an agent (often the customer) and sells them on spot or deferred payment. Available under Islamic Export Refinance Part I & II. Tijarah Learn More Tijarah enables working capital by purchasing finished goods from the customer. These are sold in the market through an agent (could be the customer) while the customer benefits from liquidity during the credit period. Istisna Learn More Istisna is used for financing manufactured goods. The Bank orders the manufacturer (customer) to produce goods as per order, takes delivery, and sells them (locally or via export) through the customer acting as the agent. Bai Salam Learn More Bai Salam Bai Salam is a Shariah-compliant alternative to bill discounting. Meezan Bank retains export bills as security and extends a Murabaha facility or buys foreign currency against PKR on Salam basis at spot rates. Musawamah Learn More Musawamah Similar to Murabaha, except the cost and profit are not disclosed to the customer. It is also allowed I & II. Long-term Financing Needs Long-term financing needs include acquiring assets like property, machinery, equipment, or vehicles. The following products cater to these needs. Diminishing Musharakah Learn More Diminishing Musharakah The Bank and customer jointly purchase an asset. The Bank leases its share to the customer, who gradually buys back the Bank’s share over time. Commonly used for assets like land, buildings, and factories. Ijarah Learn More Ijarah means leasing. The Bank acquires an asset and leases it to the customer for a fixed period. Suitable for plant, machinery, equipment, and vehicles. Trade Related Services Sight Letter of Credit Learn More Letter of Credit (under Master Musawamah Financing Agreement) Facilitates import of raw materials or assets from foreign suppliers by acting as the Bank’s agent. Usance Credit Learn More Letter of Credit Used for importing materials or assets in the customer's own name. Letter of Guarantee Learn More Letter of Guarantee A Bank guarantee to protect a third party against non-performance or payment default by the customer. Types include advance payment guarantees, performance bonds, and bid bonds. text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login اردو Rupee Current Account Open a Rupee Current Account If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Convenient and Riba Free Meezan Current Account is a Rupee-based current account that provides you the convenience of putting your money in a Riba-free account and accessing it without any restrictions on withdrawal while enjoying a host of professional conveniences from Meezan Bank. Meezan Current Account can be opened by Individuals, Sole-Proprietorships, Partnerships and Limited Companies. Freelancers can also open this account, subject to satisfactory proof of income document. Key Features No charges on Debit Card issuance: Customer can select any one card from VISA Classic, VISA Gold, Mastercard Classic, or PayPak Card (One free primary card per account) No charges on SMS Alerts subscription (1st time only) No charges on Cheque Book issuance Free Payorders, Free Online Banking, Free Internet Banking, Free E-Statements Facility, Free Digital Transaction Alert Facility (These services are also free on Meezan Bank Savings accounts). Minimum Investment for Opening Rupee Current Account is Rs. 100/- * No Maximum investment limits No restriction on withdrawals or number of transactions No deductions on low balance * No initial deposit would be required for opening of accounts for: Freelancers Mustahkeen of Zakat Students Employees of Government or semi-Government institutions for salary and pension purposes (including widows/children of deceased employees eligible for family pension/benevolent fund grant, etc.) Disclaimer: Debit Card annual charges, replacement charges, Supplementary card charges will be as per prevailing Schedule of Charges After 1st time free SMS subscription, subsequent Schedule of Charges Why Are These Services** Not Charged? **(Debit Card, Sms Alerts & Cheque Book) Current Accounts operate on the principle of Qarz, with customers acting as lenders to the Bank. The above-mentioned services assist our customers in withdrawal of funds, essentially aiding the Bank in repaying its Qarz obligations, so they can be offered with or without any charges. Note: For further clarification, please refer to clause 10/1/3 of AAOIFI Shari'ah Standard No. 19 on Loan (Qard), adopted by SBP via IBD Circular No. 01 of 2020. How the Rupee Account Works Download Fatwa Current Account is based on Qard contract where the Bank is liable to pay your money back on demand. The Bank can use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles. id: Best Bank of Pakistan | Pakistan Banking Awards 2023 | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: introduction: Meezan Bank is Pakistan’s premier Islamic bank, operating with more than 980 branches in over 320 cities across the country. The Bank has been recognized by various local and international institutes on several occasions for its phenomenal year-on-year growth and contribution to the domain of Islamic finance in the region. Meezan Bank has been acknowledged as the Best Bank of Pakistan for the third time at the Pakistan Banking Awards - 2023, the most prestigious awards in the country’s financial sector. The award ceremony is held by the Institute of Bankers Pakistan, in partnership with the Dawn Media Group and in collaboration with A.F. Ferguson and Company. The judging panel, consisting of highly respected luminaries with substantial experience in the financial sector, was chaired by Mr. Syed Salim Raza, Former Governor of the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP). The five-member jury based its decision on the banks' performance, affirming our commitment to Islamic banking and our consistent delivery of higher standards of customer service quality to further elevate service standards. The Bank was previously recognized at the same platform the Best Bank for the years 2018 and 2020. With a journey that is just 25 years old, Meezan Bank is both humbled and delighted to receive this award. We express our gratitude to all our customers for their continued patronage and trust in Islamic banking and Meezan Bank!

Teens Club Account is a Mudarabah- based relationship where you will be the Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank will be the Manager (Mudarib). Your funds will be utilized by the Bank to provide Islamic financing to customers. Meezan Bank is a keen Socially Responsible Organization and realizing this responsibility, the Teens Club Account aims to provide a forum to teens where their maturing minds are groomed in a modern yet Islamic way. The Teens Club Account provides a blend of fun learning with special focus on character and value building. Key Features Personalized Chequebook Issuance of chequebook in accordance with the Schedule of Charges (SOCs) Free Pay Orders Free Pay Orders Minimium Investment Open your account with Rs. 1000/- only Profit Higher Expected Returns Age Group Specially for teens of 12 to 18 years of age Halal Profits Halal profit every month Personalized Debit Card Free* Visa** Debit Card in different colors for boys and girls Internet and Online Banking Free Online Banking service and Internet Banking facility SMS Alerts SMS alerts on your transactions Great Gifts Exciting gifts*** on account opening * FREE for first issuance only Annual fee for subsequent years will be applicable as per SOC ** Paypak Card also available on this account, however charges as per SOC *** Gifts on account opening are subject to availability. Meezan Bank doesn't have any binding obligation to provide the gifts in case of unavailability. Key Features First ever limit based Islamic financing facility for consumer durable goods Financing based on the Shariah-compliant concept of Musawwamah. Easy Installment options for 6 to 24 months. Limit is valid for 3 years, and will be utilized for purchase of durable goods. Financing limit ranges between a Minimum of 35,000/- up to the maximum of 1,000,000/- Who is eligible for Consumer Ease Citizenship and Age Pakistani, Adult, Permanent resident Salaried 20 – 60 years old Businessmen and SEP 20 - 65 years old Minimum Income Requirements Salaried Class - 25,000/- (Gross Salary) SEP - 40,000/- Businessmen - 40,000/- Instalment amount Monthly instalment should not be more than 35% for net monthly income /- (For Salaried, SEP and Businessmen) Other Requirement Down Payment : From 5% to 50% of Market Price (T&C Apply) Tenor : 3, 6, 12 , 18, 24 months Payment Mode : Direct debit instruction (DDI) Late Payment : No Late Payment Charges Security : 3 cheques of Meezan Bank account Note: In line with State Bank of Pakistan's regulatory requirement, negative history (i.e. overdue/ late payment/ write-off/ waiver) of consumer/individual customers will be reflected in eCIB reports for two years after settlement. To show our commitment, we have established an ‘Meezan Bank's Privacy Policy’, which apprises customers of the information they will be expected to disclose, and how that information will be used by Meezan Bank. This Privacy Policy governs the manner in which MEEZAN BANK LIMITED collects, uses, maintains and discloses information collected from each user of this Web site. The terms of this disclaimer apply to both websites ( www.meezanbank.pk & www.meezanbank.com ) of Meezan Bank Limited. The Information We Collect Meezan Bank may collect personally identifiable information from Users in several ways, including online forms for ordering products and services, or applying for career opportunities or other instances where Users are invited to volunteer such information. Meezan Bank may also collect information about how Users use the Meezan Bank's Web site, by tracking the number of unique views received by the pages of the Web site or the domains from which Users originate. “Cookies" may be used to track how Users use our Web site, which is a method to store the relevant information of the Users’ PC to identify the User, should they visit the Web site again. While not all of the information that we collect from Users is personally identifiable, it may be associated with personally identifiable information that Users provide us through our Web site. How The Information is Used Meezan Bank may use personally identifiable information collected through our Web site to contact Users regarding products and services offered by the bank and otherwise to enhance Users experience with Meezan Bank. Information collected this way can also be used for research regarding the Web site’s effectiveness and the marketing, advertising and sales efforts of Meezan Bank and partner companies. Disclosure of Information Meezan Bank may disclose information collected from Users to trusted affiliates, independent contractors and business partners (if any) who will use the information for the purposes outlined above. We may also disclose aggregate, anonymous data based on from Users to investors and potential partners. Finally, we may transfer information collected in connection with a sale of our product and services. id: Sohni Dharti Remittance Account | Meezan Bank_chunk_0
text: Meezan EBiz+ LITE Login Meezan EBiz+ Login EBiz+ LITE Login EBiz+ Login If you found this page informative, share it now! Facebook LinkedIn X WhatsApp Email Print Meezan Dharti Remittance Account Where Your Hard-Earned Remittance Meets Care, Convenience & Compliance Dharti Remittance Account (MSDRA) is a Shariah-compliant account that makes sending and receiving money to your loved ones safe, easy, and worry-free. Whether you are a Non-Resident Pakistani sending funds or a beneficiary in Pakistan, Remittance Account (MSDRA) helps ensure your hard-earned money reaches your loved ones with the care, trust, and peace of mind you deserve. Key Features & Benefits No initial deposit requirement Free Pay Orders Free Issuance of Debit Card* Free Issuance of Cheque Book (for Current Accounts only) Free Mobile Banking – bank anytime, anywhere Free Online Branch Banking SMS Alerts Service as per Schedule of Charges (SOC) Profit paid on a monthly basis, calculated daily (applicable to Savings Accounts only) *Free Debit Cards include PayPak, VISA Silver, and MasterCard Silver Card. The first debit card is issued free of charge. However, Annual/Replacement/Supplementary Card charges will apply as per SOC. WHY CHOOSE MSDRA Designed for Overseas Pakistanis — Tailored exclusively to meet the needs of Pakistanis living abroad. Trusted Shariah-compliant Banking — Backed by Pakistan’s leading Islamic bank. A Secure Way to Support Your Loved Ones — Send and manage funds in Pakistan with complete confidence. How Does Dharti Remittance Account Work? Dharti Remittance Account is available both a Current Account and a Savings Account, based on the Islamic concepts of Qard and Mudarabah, respectively. Download Fatwa Mudarabah Basis By opening a MSDRA Pak Rupee Savings Account, the customer enters into a Mudarabah relationship with Meezan Bank. In this arrangement, the customer is an Investor (Rab-ul-Maal) and the Bank is the Manager (Mudarib) of the deposited funds. The Bank allocates the funds received from the customers to a deposit pool; which is then utilized to provide financing under Islamic modes including (but not limited to) Murabaha, Ijarah, Istisna and Diminishing Musharakah. Qard Basis The MSDRA Pak Rupee Current Account is based on a Qard contract. Under this arrangement, the Bank may use these funds for investment and other purposes. This account allows you the peace of mind of having your money safely deposited with a bank with the additional assurance that the Bank is not investing your money in activities that contravene Shariah principles.
